docs.title: Administrator Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive user group and privilege management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The administrator library provides comprehensive functionality for managing user groups, privileges, and administrative permissions in the Lilia framework. It handles the creation, modification, and deletion of user groups with inheritance-based privilege systems. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing privilege storage and validation while the client provides user interface elements for administrative management. It includes integration with CAMI (Comprehensive Administration Management Interface) for compatibility with other administrative systems. The library ensures proper privilege inheritance, automatic privilege registration for tools and properties, and comprehensive logging of administrative actions. It supports both console-based and GUI-based administrative command execution with proper permission checking and validation.

Setting Superadmin: To set yourself as superadmin in the console, use: plysetgroup "STEAMID" superadmin The system has three default user groups with inheritance levels: user (level 1), admin (level 2), and superadmin (level 3). Superadmin automatically has all privileges and cannot be restricted by any permission checks.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Applies kick or ban punishments to a player based on the provided parameters.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when an automated system or admin action needs to punish a player with a kick or ban.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player to punish. infraction string Description of the infraction that caused the punishment. kick boolean Whether to kick the player. ban boolean Whether to ban the player. time number Ban duration in minutes (only used if ban is true). kickKey string Localization key for kick message (defaults to "kickedForInfraction"). banKey string Localization key for ban message (defaults to "bannedForInfraction").

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Kick a player for spamming
    lia.admin.applyPunishment(player, "Spamming in chat", true, false, nil, nil, nil)
    -- Ban a player for griefing for 24 hours
    lia.admin.applyPunishment(player, "Griefing", false, true, 1440, nil, nil)
    -- Both kick and ban with custom messages
    lia.admin.applyPunishment(player, "Hacking", true, true, 10080, "kickedForHacking", "bannedForHacking")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks if a player has access to a specific privilege based on their usergroup and privilege requirements.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when verifying if a player can perform an action that requires specific permissions.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description ply **Player string** privilege string The privilege ID to check access for (e.g., "command_kick", "property_door", "tool_remover").

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true if the player has access to the privilege, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Check if player can use kick command
    if lia.admin.hasAccess(player, "command_kick") then
        -- Allow kicking
    end
    -- Check if player has access to a tool
    if lia.admin.hasAccess(player, "tool_remover") then
        -- Give tool access
    end
    -- Check usergroup access directly
    if lia.admin.hasAccess("admin", "command_ban") then
        -- Admin group has ban access
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Saves administrator group configurations and privileges to the database.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when administrator settings are modified and need to be persisted, or when syncing with clients.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description noNetwork boolean If true, skips network synchronization with clients after saving.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Save admin groups without network sync
    lia.admin.save(true)
    -- Save and sync with all clients
    lia.admin.save(false)
    -- or simply:
    lia.admin.save()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers a new privilege in the administrator system with specified access levels and categories.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when defining new administrative permissions that can be granted to user groups.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description priv table Privilege configuration table with the following fields: ID string Unique identifier for the privilege (required) Name string Display name for the privilege (optional, defaults to ID) MinAccess string Minimum usergroup required ("user", "admin", "superadmin") Category string Category for organizing privileges in menus

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Register a custom admin command privilege
    lia.admin.registerPrivilege({
        ID = "command_customban",
        Name = "Custom Ban Command",
        MinAccess = "admin",
        Category = "staffCommands"
    })
    -- Register a property privilege
    lia.admin.registerPrivilege({
        ID = "property_teleport",
        Name = "Teleport Property",
        MinAccess = "admin",
        Category = "staffPermissions"
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Removes a privilege from the administrator system and cleans up all associated data.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when a privilege is no longer needed or when cleaning up removed permissions.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The privilege ID to unregister.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Unregister a custom privilege
    lia.admin.unregisterPrivilege("command_customban")
    -- Clean up a tool privilege
    lia.admin.unregisterPrivilege("tool_remover")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Applies privilege inheritance to a usergroup, copying permissions from parent groups and ensuring appropriate access levels.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when setting up or updating usergroup permissions to ensure inheritance rules are properly applied.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description groupName string The name of the usergroup to apply inheritance to.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Apply inheritance to a moderator group
    lia.admin.applyInheritance("moderator")
    -- Apply inheritance after creating a new usergroup
    lia.admin.applyInheritance("customadmin")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads administrator group configurations from the database and initializes the admin system.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during server startup to restore saved administrator settings and permissions.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load admin system (called automatically during server initialization)
    lia.admin.load()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a new administrator usergroup with specified configuration and inheritance.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when setting up new administrator roles or permission levels in the system.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description groupName string The name of the usergroup to create. info table Optional configuration table for the group (privileges, inheritance, etc.).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Create a moderator group
    lia.admin.createGroup("moderator", {
        _info = {
            inheritance = "user",
            types = {}
        },
        command_kick = true,
        command_mute = true
    })
    -- Create a custom admin group
    lia.admin.createGroup("customadmin")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Removes an administrator usergroup from the system.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when cleaning up or removing unused administrator roles.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description groupName string The name of the usergroup to remove.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Remove a custom moderator group
    lia.admin.removeGroup("moderator")
    -- Remove a custom admin group
    lia.admin.removeGroup("customadmin")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Renames an existing administrator usergroup to a new name.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when reorganizing or rebranding administrator roles.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description oldName string The current name of the usergroup to rename. newName string The new name for the usergroup.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Rename moderator group to staff
    lia.admin.renameGroup("moderator", "staff")
    -- Rename admin group to administrator
    lia.admin.renameGroup("admin", "administrator")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sends a notification to all administrators who have permission to see admin notifications.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when important administrative events need to be communicated to staff.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description notification string The notification message key to send.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Notify admins of a potential exploit
    lia.admin.notifyAdmin("exploitDetected")
    -- Notify admins of a player report
    lia.admin.notifyAdmin("playerReportReceived")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Grants a specific permission to an administrator usergroup.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when configuring permissions for administrator roles.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description groupName string The name of the usergroup to grant the permission to. permission string The permission ID to grant. silent boolean If true, skips network synchronization.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Grant kick permission to moderators
    lia.admin.addPermission("moderator", "command_kick", false)
    -- Grant ban permission to admins silently
    lia.admin.addPermission("admin", "command_ban", true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Removes a specific permission from an administrator usergroup.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when revoking permissions from administrator roles.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description groupName string The name of the usergroup to remove the permission from. permission string The permission ID to remove. silent boolean If true, skips network synchronization.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Remove kick permission from moderators
    lia.admin.removePermission("moderator", "command_kick", false)
    -- Remove ban permission from admins silently
    lia.admin.removePermission("admin", "command_ban", true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Synchronizes administrator privileges and usergroups with clients.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when administrator data changes and needs to be updated on clients, or when a player joins.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description c Player Optional specific client to sync with. If not provided, syncs with all clients in batches.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Sync admin data with all clients
    lia.admin.sync()
    -- Sync admin data with a specific player
    lia.admin.sync(specificPlayer)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks if administrator privileges or groups have changed since the last sync.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called to determine if a sync operation is needed.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true if there are unsynced changes, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Check if admin data needs syncing
    if lia.admin.hasChanges() then
        lia.admin.sync()
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sets the usergroup of a player entity.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when promoting, demoting, or changing a player's administrative role.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description ply Player The player to change the usergroup for. newGroup string The new usergroup name to assign. source string Optional source identifier for logging.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Promote player to admin
    lia.admin.setPlayerUsergroup(player, "admin", "promotion")
    -- Demote player to user
    lia.admin.setPlayerUsergroup(player, "user", "demotion")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sets the usergroup of a player by their SteamID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when changing a player's usergroup using their SteamID, useful for offline players.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description steamId string The SteamID of the player to change the usergroup for. newGroup string The new usergroup name to assign. source string Optional source identifier for logging.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Set offline player's usergroup to admin
    lia.admin.setSteamIDUsergroup("STEAM_0:1:12345678", "admin", "promotion")
    -- Demote player by SteamID
    lia.admin.setSteamIDUsergroup("STEAM_0:1:12345678", "user", "demotion")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Executes administrative commands on players with proper permission checking and logging.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when an administrator uses commands like kick, ban, mute, gag, freeze, slay, bring, goto, etc.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description cmd string The command to execute (e.g., "kick", "ban", "mute", "gag", "freeze", "slay", "bring", "goto", "return", "jail", "cloak", "god", "ignite", "strip", "respawn", "blind"). victim **Player string** dur **number string** reason string Reason for the action (used in kick, ban, etc.). admin Player The administrator executing the command.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true if the command was executed successfully, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Kick a player for spamming
    lia.admin.serverExecCommand("kick", targetPlayer, nil, "Spamming in chat", adminPlayer)
    -- Ban a player for 24 hours
    lia.admin.serverExecCommand("ban", targetPlayer, 1440, "Griefing", adminPlayer)
    -- Mute a player
    lia.admin.serverExecCommand("mute", targetPlayer, nil, nil, adminPlayer)
    -- Bring a player to admin's position
    lia.admin.serverExecCommand("bring", targetPlayer, nil, nil, adminPlayer)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Executes an administrative command using the client-side command system.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when running admin commands through console commands or automated systems.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description cmd string The command to execute (e.g., "kick", "ban", "mute", "freeze", etc.). victim **Player string** dur number Duration for time-based commands. reason string Reason for the administrative action.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true if the command was executed successfully, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Kick a player via console command
    lia.admin.execCommand("kick", targetPlayer, nil, "Rule violation")
    -- Ban a player for 24 hours
    lia.admin.execCommand("ban", targetPlayer, 1440, "Griefing")

docs.title: Attributes Library
docs.text:
Character attribute management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The attributes library provides functionality for managing character attributes in the Lilia framework. It handles loading attribute definitions from files, registering attributes in the system, and setting up attributes for characters during spawn. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing attribute setup during character spawning and the client handling attribute-related UI elements. It includes automatic attribute loading from directories, localization support for attribute names and descriptions, and hooks for custom attribute behavior.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Discover and include attribute definitions from a directory.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During schema/gamemode startup to load all attribute files.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description directory string Path containing attribute Lua files.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load default and custom attributes.
    lia.attribs.loadFromDir(lia.plugin.getDir() .. "/attribs")
    lia.attribs.loadFromDir("schema/attribs")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register or update an attribute definition in the global list.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After loading an attribute file or when hot-reloading attributes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string Attribute key. data table Fields like name, desc, OnSetup, setup, etc.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The stored attribute table.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.attribs.register("strength", {
        name = "Strength",
        desc = "Improves melee damage and carry weight.",
        OnSetup = function(client, value)
            client:SetJumpPower(160 + value * 0.5)
        end
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Run attribute setup logic for a character on the server.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On player spawn/character load to reapply attribute effects.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Player whose character attributes are being applied.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("PlayerLoadedChar", "ApplyAttributeBonuses", function(ply)
        lia.attribs.setup(ply)
    end)

docs.title: Bars Library
docs.text:
Dynamic progress bar creation and management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The bars library provides a comprehensive system for creating and managing dynamic progress bars in the Lilia framework. It handles the creation, rendering, and lifecycle management of various types of bars including health, armor, and custom progress indicators. The library operates primarily on the client side, providing smooth animated transitions between bar values and intelligent visibility management based on value changes and user preferences. It includes built-in health and armor bars, custom action progress displays, and a flexible system for adding custom bars with priority-based ordering. The library ensures consistent visual presentation across all bar types while providing hooks for customization and integration with other framework components.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieve a registered bar definition by its identifier.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before updating/removing an existing bar or inspecting its state.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description identifier **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil Stored bar data or nil if not found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local staminaBar = lia.bar.get("stamina")
    if staminaBar then
        print("Current priority:", staminaBar.priority)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a new dynamic bar with optional priority and identifier.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Client HUD setup or when creating temporary action/status bars.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description getValue function Returns current fraction (0-1) when called. color **Color nil** priority **number nil** identifier **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Priority used for the bar.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Add a stamina bar that fades after inactivity.
    lia.bar.add(function()
        local client = LocalPlayer()
        local stamina = client:getLocalVar("stm", 100)
        return math.Clamp(stamina / 100, 0, 1)
    end, Color(120, 200, 80), 2, "stamina")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Remove a bar by its identifier.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After a timed action completes or when disabling a HUD element.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description identifier string Unique id passed during add.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: timer.Simple(5, function() lia.bar.remove("stamina") end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draw a single bar at a position with given fill and color.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Internally from drawAll or for custom bars in panels.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description pos number Current value. max number Maximum value. color Color Fill color.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Custom panel painting a download progress bar.
    function PANEL:Paint(w, h)
        lia.bar.drawBar(10, h - 24, w - 20, 16, self.progress, 1, Color(120, 180, 255))
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Show a centered action bar with text and timed progress.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: For timed actions like searching, hacking, or channeling abilities.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description text string Label to display. duration number Seconds to run before auto-removal.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("OnStartSearch", "ShowSearchBar", function(duration)
        lia.bar.drawAction(L("searchingChar"), duration)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Render all registered bars with smoothing, lifetimes, and ordering.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Each HUDPaintBackground (hooked at bottom of file).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Bars are drawn automatically via the HUDPaintBackground hook.
    -- For custom derma panels, you could manually call lia.bar.drawAll().

docs.title: Character Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive character creation, management, and persistence system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The character library provides comprehensive functionality for managing player characters in the Lilia framework. It handles character creation, loading, saving, and management across both server and client sides. The library operates character data persistence, networking synchronization, and provides hooks for character variable changes. It includes functions for character validation, database operations, inventory management, and character lifecycle management. The library ensures proper character data integrity and provides a robust system for character-based gameplay mechanics including factions, attributes, money, and custom character variables.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieve a character by ID from cache or request a load if missing.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Anytime code needs a character object by ID (selection, networking, admin tools).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description charID number Character database ID to fetch. client **Player nil** callback **function nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil The character object if already cached; otherwise nil while loading.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.char.getCharacter(targetID, ply, function(char)
        if char then
            char:sync(ply)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Return a table of all players currently holding loaded characters.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: For admin panels, diagnostics, or mass operations over active characters.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Keyed by Player with values of their active character objects.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
for ply, char in pairs(lia.char.getAll()) do
        print(ply:Name(), char:getName())
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Check if a character ID currently exists in the local cache.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before loading or accessing a character to avoid duplicate work.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description charID number Character database ID to test.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if the character is cached, otherwise false.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if not lia.char.isLoaded(id) then
        lia.char.getCharacter(id)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Insert a character into the cache and resolve any pending requests.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After successfully loading or creating a character object.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id number Character database ID. character table Character object to store.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.addCharacter(char:getID(), char)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Remove a character from the local cache.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After a character is deleted, unloaded, or no longer needed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id number Character database ID to remove.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.removeCharacter(charID)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Construct a character object and populate its variables with provided data or defaults.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During character creation or when rebuilding a character from stored data.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description data table Raw character data keyed by variable name. id **number nil** client **Player nil** steamID **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table New character object.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local char = lia.char.new(row, row.id, ply)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a hook function that runs when a specific character variable changes.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When modules need to react to updates of a given character variable.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description varName string Character variable name. hookName string Unique identifier for the hook. func function Callback invoked with (character, oldValue, newValue).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.char.hookVar("money", "OnMoneyChanged", function(char, old, new)
        hook.Run("OnCharMoneyChanged", char, old, new)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a character variable and generate accessor/mutator helpers with optional networking.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During schema load to declare character fields such as name, money, or custom data.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Variable identifier. data table Configuration table defining defaults, validation, networking, and callbacks.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.char.registerVar("title", {
        field = "title",
        fieldType = "string",
        default = "",
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Read character data key/value pairs stored in the chardata table.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When modules need arbitrary persisted data for a character, optionally scoped to a single key.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description charID number Character database ID to query. key **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|any|nil Table of all key/value pairs, a single value when key is provided, or nil if not found/invalid.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local prestige = lia.char.getCharData(charID, "prestige")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieve raw character data from chardata without touching the cache.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When a direct database read is needed, bypassing any loaded character state.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description charID number Character database ID to query. key **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any|table|false|nil Decoded value for the key, a table of all key/value pairs, false if a keyed lookup is missing, or nil on invalid input.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local allData = lia.char.getCharDataRaw(charID)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Find the player entity that owns a given character ID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When needing to target or notify the current owner of a loaded character.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description ID number Character database ID.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Player|nil Player who currently has the character loaded, or nil if none.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local owner = lia.char.getOwnerByID(charID)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Get the active character of an online player by SteamID/SteamID64.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: For lookups across connected players when only a Steam identifier is known.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description steamID string SteamID or SteamID64 string.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil Character object if the player is online and has one loaded, else nil.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local char = lia.char.getBySteamID(targetSteamID)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Return the team/class color for a player, falling back to team color.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Whenever UI or drawing code needs a consistent color for the player's current class.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Player whose color is requested.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Color table sourced from class definition or team color.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local color = lia.char.getTeamColor(ply)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Create a new character row, build its object, and initialize inventories.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During character creation after validation to persist and ready the new character.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description data table Prepared character data including steamID, faction, and name. callback **function nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.create(payload, function(charID) print("created", charID) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Load all characters for a player (or a specific ID) into memory and inventory.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On player connect or when an admin requests to restore a specific character.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Player whose characters should be loaded. callback **function nil** id **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.restore(ply, function(chars) print("loaded", #chars) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Unload and save all characters cached for a player.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When a player disconnects or is cleaned up to free memory and inventories.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Player whose cached characters should be unloaded.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.cleanUpForPlayer(ply)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Delete a character, its data, and inventories, and notify affected players.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: By admin or player actions that permanently remove a character.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id number Character database ID to delete. client **Player nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.delete(charID, ply)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Check the ban state of a character in the database.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before allowing a character to load or when displaying ban info.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description charID number Character database ID.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number|nil Ban flag/value (0 if not banned), or nil on invalid input.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: if lia.char.getCharBanned(id) ~= 0 then return end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Write a character variable to the database and update any loaded instance.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Whenever persistent character fields or custom data need to be changed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description charID number Character database ID. field string Character var or custom data key. value any Value to store; nil removes custom data entries.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True on success, false on immediate failure.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.setCharDatabase(charID, "money", newAmount)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Save and unload a character from memory, clearing associated data vars.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When a character is no longer active or needs to be freed from cache.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description charID number Character database ID to unload.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if a character was unloaded, false if none was loaded.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.unloadCharacter(charID)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Unload all cached characters for a player except the currently active one.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After character switches to reduce memory and inventory usage.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Player whose cached characters should be reduced. activeCharID number Character ID to keep loaded.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Count of characters that were unloaded.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.unloadUnusedCharacters(ply, newCharID)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Load a single character from the database, building inventories and caching it.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When a specific character is selected, restored, or fetched server-side.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description charID number Character database ID to load. client **Player nil** callback **function nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.char.loadSingleCharacter(id, ply, function(char) if char then char:sync(ply) end end)

docs.title: Chatbox Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive chat system management with message routing and formatting for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The chatbox library provides comprehensive functionality for managing chat systems in the Lilia framework. It handles registration of different chat types (IC, OOC, whisper, etc.), message parsing and routing, distance-based hearing mechanics, and chat formatting. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing message distribution and validation, while the client handles parsing and display formatting. It includes support for anonymous messaging, custom prefixes, radius-based communication, and integration with the command system for chat-based commands.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Prepend a timestamp to chat messages based on option settings.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During chat display formatting (client) to show the time.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description ooc boolean Whether the chat is OOC (affects spacing).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Timestamp text or empty string.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared (used clientside)

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: chat.AddText(lia.chat.timestamp(false), Color(255,255,255), message)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a chat class (IC/OOC/whisper/custom) with prefixes and rules.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On initialization to add new chat types and bind aliases/commands.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description chatType string data table Fields: prefix, radius/onCanHear, onCanSay, format, color, arguments, etc.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared (prefix commands created clientside)

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.chat.register("yell", {
        prefix = {"/y", "/yell"},
        radius = 600,
        format = "chatYellFormat",
        arguments = {{name = "message", type = "string"}},
        onChatAdd = function(speaker, text) chat.AddText(Color(255,200,120), "[Y] ", speaker:Name(), ": ", text) end
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Parse a raw chat message to determine chat type, strip prefixes, and send.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On client (local send) and server (routing) before dispatching chat.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player message string noSend **boolean nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string, string, boolean chatType, message, anonymous

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- client
    lia.chat.parse(LocalPlayer(), "/y Hello there!")
    -- server hook
    hook.Add("PlayerSay", "LiliaChatParse", function(ply, txt)
        if lia.chat.parse(ply, txt) then return "" end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Send a chat message to eligible listeners, honoring canHear/canSay rules.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Server-side after parsing chat or programmatic chat generation.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description speaker Player chatType string text string anonymous boolean receivers **table nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.chat.send(ply, "ic", "Hello world", false)

docs.title: Classes Library
docs.text:
Character class management and validation system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The classes library provides comprehensive functionality for managing character classes in the Lilia framework. It handles registration, validation, and management of player classes within factions. The library operates on both server and client sides, allowing for dynamic class creation, whitelist management, and player class assignment validation. It includes functionality for loading classes from directories, checking class availability, retrieving class information, and managing class limits. The library ensures proper faction validation and provides hooks for custom class behavior and restrictions.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers or updates a class definition within the global class list.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Invoked during schema initialization or dynamic class creation to ensure a class entry exists before use.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string Unique identifier for the class; must be consistent across loads. data table Class metadata such as name, desc, faction, limit, OnCanBe, etc.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The registered class table with applied defaults.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.class.register("soldier", {
        name = "Soldier",
        faction = FACTION_MILITARY,
        limit = 4
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads and registers all class definitions from a directory.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Used during schema loading to automatically include class files in a folder following the naming convention.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description directory string Path to the directory containing class Lua files.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Operates for side effects of registering classes.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.class.loadFromDir("lilia/gamemode/classes")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Determines whether a client can join a specific class.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Checked before class selection to enforce faction, limits, whitelist, and custom restrictions.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Player attempting to join the class. class **number string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean|string False and a reason string on failure; otherwise returns the class's isDefault value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local ok, reason = lia.class.canBe(ply, CLASS_CITIZEN)
    if ok then
        -- proceed with class change
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves a class table by index or unique identifier.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Used whenever class metadata is needed given a known identifier.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description identifier **number string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil The class table if found; otherwise nil.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local classData = lia.class.get("soldier")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Collects all players currently assigned to the given class.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Used when enforcing limits or displaying membership lists.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description class **number string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Array of player entities in the class.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
for _, ply in ipairs(lia.class.getPlayers("soldier")) do
        -- notify class members
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Counts how many players are in the specified class.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Used to check class limits or display class population.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description class **number string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Current number of players in the class.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local count = lia.class.getPlayerCount(CLASS_ENGINEER)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Finds the class index by matching uniqueID or display name.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Used to resolve user input to a class entry before further lookups.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description class string Text to match against class uniqueID or name.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number|nil The class index if a match is found; otherwise nil.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local idx = lia.class.retrieveClass("Engineer")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks whether a class uses whitelist access.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Queried before allowing class selection or displaying class info.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description class **number string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if the class is whitelisted and not default; otherwise false.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if lia.class.hasWhitelist(CLASS_PILOT) then
        -- restrict to whitelisted players
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Returns a list of classes the provided client is allowed to join.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Used to build class selection menus and enforce availability.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client **Player nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Array of class tables the client can currently join.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local options = lia.class.retrieveJoinable(ply)

docs.title: Color Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive color and theme management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The color library provides comprehensive functionality for managing colors and themes in the Lilia framework. It handles color registration, theme management, color manipulation, and smooth theme transitions. The library operates primarily on the client side, with theme registration available on both server and client. It includes predefined color names, theme switching capabilities, color adjustment functions, and smooth animated transitions between themes. The library ensures consistent color usage across the entire gamemode interface and provides tools for creating custom themes and color schemes.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a named color so string-based Color() calls can resolve it.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During client initialization or when adding palette entries at runtime.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Identifier stored in lowercase. color **table Color**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.color.register("warning", Color(255, 140, 0))
    local c = Color("warning")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Apply additive offsets to a color to quickly tint or shade it.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: While building UI states (hover/pressed) or computing theme variants.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description color Color Base color. aOffset **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Color Adjusted color.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local base = lia.color.getMainColor()
    button:SetTextColor(lia.color.adjust(base, -40, -20, -60))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Darken a color by a fractional factor.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Deriving hover/pressed backgrounds from a base accent color.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description color Color Base color to darken. factor **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Color Darkened color.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local accent = lia.color.getMainColor()
    local pressed = lia.color.darken(accent, 0.2)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Get the active theme id from config in lowercase.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before looking up theme tables or theme-specific assets.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Lowercased theme id (default "teal").

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if lia.color.getCurrentTheme() == "dark" then
        panel:SetDarkMode(true)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Get the display name of the currently selected theme.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Showing UI labels or logs about the active theme.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Theme name from config with original casing.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: chat.AddText(Color(180, 220, 255), "Theme: ", lia.color.getCurrentThemeName())

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Fetch the main color from the current theme with sensible fallbacks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Setting accent colors for buttons, bars, and highlights.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Color Main theme color, falling back to the default theme or teal.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local accent = lia.color.getMainColor()
    button:SetTextColor(accent)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Apply a theme immediately or begin a smooth transition toward it, falling back to Teal/default palettes and firing OnThemeChanged after updates.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On config changes, theme selection menus, or client startup.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description themeName **string nil** useTransition **boolean nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
concommand.Add("lia_theme_preview", function(_, _, args)
        lia.color.applyTheme(args[1] or "Teal", true)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Check whether a theme transition is currently blending.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: To avoid overlapping transitions or to gate UI animations.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if a transition is active, otherwise false.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if lia.color.isTransitionActive() then return end
    lia.color.applyTheme("Dark", true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Convenience wrapper to start a theme transition immediately.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: From theme preview buttons to animate a swap.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description themeName string Target theme id.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
vgui.Create("DButton").DoClick = function()
        lia.color.testThemeTransition("Red")
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Begin blending from the current palette toward a target theme, falling back to Teal when missing and finishing by firing OnThemeChanged once applied.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Inside applyTheme when transitions are enabled or via previews.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Theme id to blend toward.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.color.transition.speed = 1.5
    lia.color.startThemeTransition("Ice")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Determine whether a value resembles a Color table.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: While blending themes to decide how to lerp entries.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description v any Value to test.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True when v has numeric r, g, b, a fields.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if lia.color.isColor(entry) then
        panel:SetTextColor(entry)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Build a readable contrasting color (alpha 255) based on a main color.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Choosing text or negative colors for overlays and highlights.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description mainColor **Color nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Color Contrasting color tuned for readability.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local negative = lia.color.calculateNegativeColor()
    frame:SetTextColor(negative)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Derive a suite of adjusted colors from the main theme color, including brightness-aware text and a calculated negative color.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Building consistent palettes for backgrounds, accents, and text.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Contains background, sidebar, accent, text, hover, border, highlight, negative.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local palette = lia.color.returnMainAdjustedColors()
    panel:SetBGColor(palette.background)
    panel:SetTextColor(palette.text)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: FrameTime-scaled color lerp helper.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Theme transitions or animated highlights needing smooth color changes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description frac number Multiplier applied to FrameTime for lerp speed. col1 Color Source color; defaults to white when nil. col2 Color Target color; defaults to white when nil.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Color Interpolated color.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local blink = lia.color.lerp(6, Color(255, 0, 0), Color(255, 255, 255))
    panel:SetBorderColor(blink)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a theme table by name for later selection.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During initialization to expose custom palettes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Theme name/id; stored in lowercase. themeData table Map of color keys to Color values or arrays.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.color.registerTheme("MyStudio", {
        maincolor = Color(120, 200, 255),
        background = Color(20, 24, 32),
        text = Color(230, 240, 255)
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Return a sorted list of available theme ids.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: To populate config dropdowns or theme selection menus.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Sorted array of theme ids.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local options = {}
    for _, id in ipairs(lia.color.getAllThemes()) do
        options[#options + 1] = id
    end

docs.title: Commands Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive command registration, parsing, and execution system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The commands library provides comprehensive functionality for managing and executing commands in the Lilia framework. It handles command registration, argument parsing, access control, privilege management, and command execution across both server and client sides. The library supports complex argument types including players, booleans, strings, and tables, with automatic syntax generation and validation. It integrates with the administrator system for privilege-based access control and provides user interface elements for command discovery and argument prompting. The library ensures secure command execution with proper permission checks and logging capabilities.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Generate a human-readable syntax string from a list of argument definitions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During command registration to populate data.syntax for menus and help text.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description args table Array of argument tables {name=, type=, optional=}.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Concatenated syntax tokens describing the command arguments.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local syntax = lia.command.buildSyntaxFromArguments({
        {name = "target", type = "player"},
        {name = "amount", type = "number", optional = true}
    })
    -- "[player target] [string amount optional]"

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a command and normalize its metadata, syntax, privileges, aliases, and callbacks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During schema or module initialization to expose new chat/console commands.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description command string Unique command key. data table Command definition (arguments, desc, privilege, superAdminOnly, adminOnly, alias, onRun, onCheckAccess, etc.).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.command.add("bring", {
        desc = "Bring a player to you.",
        adminOnly = true,
        arguments = {
            {name = "target", type = "player"}
        },
        onRun = function(client, args)
            local target = lia.command.findPlayer(args[1])
            if IsValid(target) then target:SetPos(client:GetPos() + client:GetForward() * 50) end
        end
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Determine whether a client may run a command based on privileges, hooks, faction/class access, and custom checks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before executing a command or showing it in help menus.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Player requesting access. command string Command name to check. data **table nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean, string allowed result and privilege name for UI/feedback.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local canUse, priv = lia.command.hasAccess(ply, "bring")
    if not canUse then ply:notifyErrorLocalized("noPerm") end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Split a raw command string into arguments while preserving quoted segments.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When parsing chat-entered commands before validation or prompting.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description text string Raw command text excluding the leading slash.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Array of parsed arguments.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local args = lia.command.extractArgs("'John Doe' 250")
    -- {"John Doe", "250"}

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Execute a registered command for a given client with arguments and emit post-run hooks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Player whose chat is being parsed. text string Full chat text. realCommand **string nil** arguments **table nil** Pre unknown parsed arguments; when nil they are derived from text. Pre unknown parsed arguments; when nil they are derived from text.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true if the text was handled as a command.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

hook.Add("PlayerSay", "liaChatCommands", function(ply, text)

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: Configuration Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive user-configurable settings management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The configuration library provides comprehensive functionality for managing user-configurable settings in the Lilia framework. It handles the creation, storage, retrieval, and persistence of various types of options including boolean toggles, numeric sliders, color pickers, text inputs, and dropdown selections. The library operates on both client and server sides, with automatic persistence to JSON files and optional networking capabilities for server-side options. It includes a complete user interface system for displaying and modifying options through the configuration menu, with support for categories, visibility conditions, and real-time updates. The library ensures that all user preferences are maintained across sessions and provides hooks for modules to react to option changes.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a config entry with defaults, UI metadata, and optional callback.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During schema/module initialization to expose server-stored configuration.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Unique identifier for the config entry. name string Display text or localization key for UI. value any Default value; type inferred when data.type is omitted. callback **function nil** data table Fields such as type, desc, category, options/optionsFunc, noNetworking, etc.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.config.add("MaxThirdPersonDistance", "maxThirdPersonDistance", 100, function(old, new)
        lia.option.set("thirdPersonDistance", math.min(lia.option.get("thirdPersonDistance", new), new))
    end, {category = "categoryGameplay", type = "Int", min = 10, max = 200})

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Resolve a config entry's selectable options, static list or generated.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before rendering dropdown-type configs or validating submitted values.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Config key to resolve options for.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Options array or key/value table; empty when unavailable.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local opts = lia.config.getOptions("Theme")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Override the default value for an already registered config entry.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During migrations, schema overrides, or backward-compatibility fixes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Config key to override. value any New default value.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.config.setDefault("StartingMoney", 300)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Force-set a config value and fire update hooks without networking.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Runtime adjustments (admin tools/commands) or hot reload scenarios.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Config key to change. value any Value to assign. noSave **boolean nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.config.forceSet("MaxCharacters", 10, false)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Set a config value, fire update hooks, run server callbacks, network to clients, and persist.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Through admin tools/commands or internal code updating configuration.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Config key to change. value any Value to assign and broadcast.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.config.set("RunSpeed", 420)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieve a config value with fallback to its stored default or a provided default.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Anywhere configuration influences gameplay or UI logic.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Config key to read. default any Optional fallback when no stored value or default exists.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any Stored value, default value, or supplied fallback.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local walkSpeed = lia.config.get("WalkSpeed", 200)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Load config values from the database (server) or request them from the server (client).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On initialization to hydrate lia.config.stored after database connectivity.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Server path is asynchronous; client path simply sends a request.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: hook.Add("DatabaseConnected", "LoadLiliaConfig", lia.config.load)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Collect config entries whose values differ from last synced values or their defaults.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Prior to sending incremental config updates to clients.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description includeDefaults **boolean nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table key → value for configs that changed.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local changed = lia.config.getChangedValues()
    if next(changed) then lia.config.send() end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Check whether any config values differ from the last synced snapshot.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: To determine if a resync to clients is required.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True when at least one config value has changed.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: if lia.config.hasChanges() then lia.config.send() end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Send config values to one player (full payload) or broadcast only changed values.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After config changes or when a player joins the server.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client **Player nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("PlayerInitialSpawn", "SyncConfig", function(ply) lia.config.send(ply) end)
    lia.config.send() -- broadcast diffs

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Persist all config values to the database.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After changes, on shutdown, or during scheduled saves.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.config.save()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Reset all config values to defaults, then save and sync to clients.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During admin resets or troubleshooting.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.config.reset()

docs.title: Currency Library
docs.text:
In-game currency formatting, display, and management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The currency library provides comprehensive functionality for managing in-game currency within the Lilia framework. It handles currency formatting, display, and physical money entity spawning. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server handling money entity creation and spawning, while the client handles currency display formatting. It includes localization support for currency names and symbols, ensuring proper pluralization and formatting based on amount values. The library integrates with the configuration system to allow customizable currency symbols and names.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Format a numeric amount into a localized currency string with the configured symbol and singular/plural name.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Whenever a currency amount needs to be shown to players or logged (UI, chat, logs, tooltips).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description amount number Raw amount to format; must be a number.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Formatted amount with symbol prefix and the singular or plural currency name.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
chat.AddText(L("youReceivedMoney", lia.currency.get(250)))
    lia.log.add(client, "moneyPickedUp", 250)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Spawn a physical money entity at a world position and assign it an amount.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Server-side when creating droppable currency (player drops, rewards, refunds, scripted events).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description pos Vector World position to spawn the money entity; required. amount number Currency amount to store on the entity; must be non-negative. angle **Angle nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Entity|nil Created lia_money entity, or nil if input is invalid or entity creation fails.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("OnNPCKilled", "DropBountyCash", function(npc, attacker)
        if not IsValid(attacker) or not attacker:IsPlayer() then return end
        local money = lia.currency.spawn(npc:GetPos() + Vector(0, 0, 10), math.random(50, 150))
        if IsValid(money) then
            money:SetVelocity(VectorRand() * 80)
        end
    end)

docs.title: DarkRP Compatibility Library
docs.text: The DarkRP compatibility library provides essential functions for maintaining compatibility

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Determine whether a position is free of solid contents, players, NPCs, or props.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before spawning DarkRP-style shipments or entities to ensure the destination is clear.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description position Vector World position to test. entitiesToIgnore **table nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true when the spot contains no blocking contents or entities; otherwise false.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local spawnPos = ent:GetPos() + Vector(0, 0, 16)
    if lia.darkrp.isEmpty(spawnPos) then
        lia.darkrp.createEntity("Ammo Crate", {ent = "item_ammo_crate", model = "models/Items/ammocrate_smg1.mdl"})
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Locate the nearest empty position around a starting point within a search radius.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Selecting safe fallback positions for DarkRP-style shipments or NPC spawns.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description startPos Vector Origin position to test first. entitiesToIgnore **table nil** maxDistance number Maximum distance to search away from the origin. searchStep number Increment used when expanding the search radius. checkArea Vector Additional offset tested to ensure enough clearance (for hull height, etc.).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Vector First empty position discovered; if none found, returns startPos.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local spawnPos = lia.darkrp.findEmptyPos(requestPos, nil, 256, 16, Vector(0, 0, 32))
    npc:SetPos(spawnPos)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Provide a DarkRP-compatible wrapper for Lilia's localized notify system.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: From DarkRP addons or compatibility code that expects DarkRP.notify to exist.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Recipient of the notification. notifyType number Unused legacy parameter kept for API parity. duration number Unused legacy parameter kept for API parity. message string Localization key or message to pass to Lilia's notifier.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.darkrp.notify(ply, NOTIFY_GENERIC, 4, "You received a paycheck.")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Wrap long text to a maximum line width based on the active surface font metrics.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Preparing DarkRP-compatible messages for HUD or chat rendering without overflow.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description text string Message to wrap. fontName string Name of the font to measure. maxLineWidth number Maximum pixel width allowed per line before inserting a newline.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Wrapped text with newline characters inserted.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local wrapped = lia.darkrp.textWrap("A very long notice message...", "DermaDefault", 240)
    chat.AddText(color_white, wrapped)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Format a currency amount using Lilia's currency system while matching DarkRP's API.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Anywhere DarkRP.formatMoney is expected by compatibility layers or addons.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description amount number Currency amount to format.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Localized and formatted currency string.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local paycheck = DarkRP.formatMoney(500)
    chat.AddText(L("paydayReceived", paycheck))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a DarkRP entity definition as a Lilia item for compatibility.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: While converting DarkRP shipments or entities into Lilia items at load time.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Display name for the item. data table Supported fields: cmd, ent, model, desc, price, category.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.darkrp.createEntity("Ammo Crate", {
        ent = "item_ammo_crate",
        model = "models/Items/ammocrate_smg1.mdl",
        price = 750,
        category = "Supplies"
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Provide an API stub for DarkRP category creation.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Invoked by DarkRP.createCategory during addon initialization; intentionally no-op.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- API parity only; this function performs no actions.
    lia.darkrp.createCategory()

docs.title: Data Library
docs.text:
Data persistence, serialization, and management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The data library provides comprehensive functionality for data persistence, serialization, and management within the Lilia framework. It handles encoding and decoding of complex data types including vectors, angles, colors, and nested tables for database storage. The library manages both general data storage with gamemode and map-specific scoping, as well as entity persistence for maintaining spawned entities across server restarts. It includes automatic serialization/deserialization, database integration, and caching mechanisms to ensure efficient data access and storage operations.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Encode vectors/angles/colors/tables into JSON-safe structures.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before persisting data to DB or file storage.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description value any

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any Encoded representation.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local payload = lia.data.encodetable({
        pos = Vector(0, 0, 64),
        ang = Angle(0, 90, 0),
        tint = Color(255, 0, 0)
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Decode nested structures into native types (Vector/Angle/Color).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After reading serialized data from DB/file.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description value any

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any Decoded value with deep conversion.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local decoded = lia.data.decode(storedJsonTable)
    local pos = decoded.spawnPos

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Serialize a value into JSON, pre-encoding special types.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before writing data blobs into DB columns.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description value any

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string JSON string.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local json = lia.data.serialize({pos = Vector(1,2,3)})
    lia.db.updateSomewhere(json)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Deserialize JSON/pon or raw tables back into native types.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After fetching data rows from DB.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description raw **string table

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local row = lia.db.select(...):get()
    local data = lia.data.deserialize(row.data)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Decode a vector from various string/table encodings.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: While rebuilding persistent entities or map data.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description raw any

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Vector|any

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local pos = lia.data.decodeVector(row.pos)
    if isvector(pos) then ent:SetPos(pos) end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Decode an angle from string/table encodings.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During persistence loading or data deserialization.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description raw any

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Angle|any

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local ang = lia.data.decodeAngle(row.angles)
    if isangle(ang) then ent:SetAngles(ang) end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string global **boolean nil** ignoreMap **boolean nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.text: Server

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.text: • promise

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Load persistent entities from DB, decode fields, and cache them.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description callback **function nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • promise

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.data.loadPersistenceData(function(entities)
        for _, entData in ipairs(entities) do
            -- spawn logic here
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Fetch a stored key from cache, deserializing strings on demand.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Anywhere stored data is read after loadTables.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string default any

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.text: Shared

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
