docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: Database Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive database management system with SQLite support for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The database library provides comprehensive database management functionality for the Lilia framework. It handles all database operations including connection management, table creation and modification, data insertion, updates, queries, and schema management. The library supports SQLite as the primary database engine with extensible module support for other database systems. It includes advanced features such as prepared statements, transactions, bulk operations, data type conversion, and database snapshots for backup and restore operations. The library ensures data persistence across server restarts and provides robust error handling with deferred promise-based operations for asynchronous database queries. It manages core gamemode tables for players, characters, inventories, items, configuration, logs, and administrative data while supporting dynamic schema modifications.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Establishes a connection to the database using the configured database module and initializes the query system.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during gamemode initialization to set up the database connection, or when reconnecting to the database.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description callback function Optional callback function to execute when the connection is established. reconnect boolean Optional flag to force reconnection even if already connected.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.connect(function()
        print("Database connected successfully!")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Completely removes all Lilia-related database tables from the database.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when performing a complete data wipe/reset of the gamemode's database.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description callback function Optional callback function to execute when all tables have been wiped.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.wipeTables(function()
        print("All Lilia tables have been wiped!")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates all core Lilia database tables if they don't exist, including tables for players, characters, inventories, items, configuration, logs, and administrative data.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during gamemode initialization to set up the database schema and prepare the database for use.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.db.loadTables()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Returns a deferred promise that resolves when all database tables have finished loading.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when code needs to wait for database tables to be fully initialized before proceeding with database operations.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves when tables are loaded.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.waitForTablesToLoad():next(function()
        -- Database tables are now ready
        lia.db.select("*", "characters"):next(function(results)
            print("Characters loaded:", #results)
        end)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Converts Lua data types to SQL-compatible string formats for database queries.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Automatically called when building SQL queries to ensure proper data type conversion.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description value any The value to convert (string, number, boolean, table, nil). noEscape boolean Optional flag to skip SQL escaping for strings and tables.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string The converted value as a SQL-compatible string.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local sqlValue = lia.db.convertDataType("John's Name")
    -- Returns: "'John''s Name'" (properly escaped)
    local sqlValue2 = lia.db.convertDataType({health = 100})
    -- Returns: "'{\"health\":100}'" (JSON encoded and escaped)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Inserts a new row into the specified database table with the provided data.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when adding new records to database tables such as characters, items, or player data.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description value table Table containing column-value pairs to insert. callback function Optional callback function called with (results, lastID) when the query completes. dbTable string Optional table name (defaults to "characters" if not specified).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with {results = results, lastID = lastID}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.insertTable({
        steamID = "STEAM_0:1:12345678",
        name = "John Doe",
        money = "1000"
    }, function(results, lastID)
        print("Character created with ID:", lastID)
    end, "characters"):next(function(result)
        print("Insert completed, last ID:", result.lastID)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Updates existing rows in the specified database table with the provided data.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when modifying existing records in database tables such as updating character data or player information.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description value table Table containing column-value pairs to update. callback function Optional callback function called with (results, lastID) when the query completes. dbTable string Optional table name without "lia_" prefix (defaults to "characters" if not specified). condition **table string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with {results = results, lastID = lastID}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Update character money
    lia.db.updateTable({
        money = "500"
    }, nil, "characters", {id = 123}):next(function()
        print("Character money updated")
    end)
    -- Update with string condition
    lia.db.updateTable({
        lastJoin = os.date("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    }, nil, "players", "steamID = 'STEAM_0:1:12345678'")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Selects data from the specified database table with optional conditions and limits.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when retrieving data from database tables such as fetching character information or player data.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description fields **string table** dbTable string Table name without "lia_" prefix (defaults to "characters" if not specified). condition **table string** limit number Optional LIMIT clause to restrict number of results.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with {results = results, lastID = lastID}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Select all characters
    lia.db.select("*", "characters"):next(function(result)
        print("Found", #result.results, "characters")
    end)
    -- Select specific fields with condition
    lia.db.select({"name", "money"}, "characters", {steamID = "STEAM_0:1:12345678"}):next(function(result)
        for _, char in ipairs(result.results) do
            print(char.name, "has", char.money, "money")
        end
    end)
    -- Select with limit
    lia.db.select("name", "characters", nil, 5):next(function(result)
        print("First 5 characters:")
        for _, char in ipairs(result.results) do
            print("-", char.name)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Selects data from the specified database table with complex conditions and optional ordering.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing advanced query conditions with operator support and ordering.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description fields **string table** dbTable string Table name without "lia_" prefix. conditions **table string** limit number Optional LIMIT clause to restrict number of results. orderBy string Optional ORDER BY clause for sorting results.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with {results = results, lastID = lastID}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Select with complex conditions and ordering
    lia.db.selectWithCondition("*", "characters", {
        money = {operator = ">", value = 1000},
        faction = "citizen"
    }, 10, "name ASC"):next(function(result)
        print("Found", #result.results, "rich citizens")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Counts the number of rows in the specified database table that match the given condition.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing to determine how many records exist in a table, such as counting characters or items.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description dbTable string Table name without "lia_" prefix. condition **table string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with the count as a number.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Count all characters
    lia.db.count("characters"):next(function(count)
        print("Total characters:", count)
    end)
    -- Count characters for a specific player
    lia.db.count("characters", {steamID = "STEAM_0:1:12345678"}):next(function(count)
        print("Player has", count, "characters")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Dynamically adds missing database fields to the characters table based on character variable definitions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during database initialization to ensure all character variables have corresponding database columns.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.db.addDatabaseFields()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks if any records exist in the specified table that match the given condition.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called to verify the existence of records before performing operations.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description dbTable string Table name without "lia_" prefix. condition **table string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves to true if records exist, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.exists("characters", {steamID = "STEAM_0:1:12345678"}):next(function(exists)
        if exists then
            print("Player has characters")
        else
            print("Player has no characters")
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Selects a single row from the specified database table that matches the given condition.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when retrieving a specific record from database tables, such as finding a character by ID.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description fields **string table** dbTable string Table name without "lia_" prefix. condition **table string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with the first matching row as a table, or nil if no rows found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Get character by ID
    lia.db.selectOne("*", "characters", {id = 123}):next(function(character)
        if character then
            print("Found character:", character.name)
        else
            print("Character not found")
        end
    end)
    -- Get player data by SteamID
    lia.db.selectOne({"name", "money"}, "players", {steamID = "STEAM_0:1:12345678"}):next(function(player)
        if player then
            print(player.name, "has", player.money, "money")
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Inserts multiple rows into the specified database table in a single query for improved performance.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when inserting large amounts of data at once, such as bulk importing items or characters.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description dbTable string Table name without "lia_" prefix. rows table Array of row data tables, where each table contains column-value pairs.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves when the bulk insert completes.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local items = {
        {uniqueID = "item1", invID = 1, quantity = 5},
        {uniqueID = "item2", invID = 1, quantity = 3},
        {uniqueID = "item3", invID = 2, quantity = 1}
    }
    lia.db.bulkInsert("items", items):next(function()
        print("Bulk insert completed")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Performs bulk insert or replace operations on multiple rows in a single query.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when bulk updating/inserting data where existing records should be replaced.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description dbTable string Table name without "lia_" prefix. rows table Array of row data tables to insert or replace.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves when the bulk upsert completes.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local data = {
        {id = 1, name = "Item 1", value = 100},
        {id = 2, name = "Item 2", value = 200}
    }
    lia.db.bulkUpsert("custom_items", data):next(function()
        print("Bulk upsert completed")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Inserts a new row into the database table, but ignores the operation if a conflict occurs (such as duplicate keys).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when inserting data that might already exist, where duplicates should be silently ignored.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description value table Table containing column-value pairs to insert. dbTable string Optional table name without "lia_" prefix (defaults to "characters").

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with {results = results, lastID = lastID}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.insertOrIgnore({
        steamID = "STEAM_0:1:12345678",
        name = "Player Name"
    }, "players"):next(function(result)
        print("Player record inserted or already exists")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks if a database table with the specified name exists.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called before performing operations on tables to verify they exist, or when checking schema state.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description tbl string The table name to check for existence.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves to true if the table exists, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.tableExists("lia_characters"):next(function(exists)
        if exists then
            print("Characters table exists")
        else
            print("Characters table does not exist")
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks if a column/field with the specified name exists in the given database table.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called before accessing table columns to verify they exist, or when checking schema modifications.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description tbl string The table name to check (should include "lia_" prefix). field string The field/column name to check for existence.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves to true if the field exists, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.fieldExists("lia_characters", "custom_field"):next(function(exists)
        if exists then
            print("Custom field exists")
        else
            print("Custom field does not exist")
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves a list of all Lilia database tables (tables starting with "lia_").

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing to enumerate all database tables, such as for maintenance operations or schema inspection.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with an array of table names.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.getTables():next(function(tables)
        print("Lilia tables:")
        for _, tableName in ipairs(tables) do
            print("-", tableName)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Executes multiple database queries as an atomic transaction - either all queries succeed or all are rolled back.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when performing multiple related database operations that must be atomic, such as transferring items between inventories.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description queries table Array of SQL query strings to execute in sequence.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves when the transaction completes successfully, or rejects if any query fails.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local queries = {
        "UPDATE lia_characters SET money = money - 100 WHERE id = 1",
        "UPDATE lia_characters SET money = money + 100 WHERE id = 2",
        "INSERT INTO lia_logs (message) VALUES ('Money transfer completed')"
    }
    lia.db.transaction(queries):next(function()
        print("Transaction completed successfully")
    end):catch(function(err)
        print("Transaction failed:", err)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Escapes SQL identifiers (table and column names) by wrapping them in backticks and escaping any backticks within.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Automatically called when building SQL queries to safely handle identifiers that might contain special characters.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier (table name, column name, etc.) to escape.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string The escaped identifier wrapped in backticks.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local escaped = lia.db.escapeIdentifier("user_name")
    -- Returns: "`user_name`"
    local escaped2 = lia.db.escapeIdentifier("table`with`ticks")
    -- Returns: "`table``with``ticks`"

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Inserts a new row into the database table, or replaces the existing row if it already exists (SQLite UPSERT operation).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when you want to ensure a record exists with specific data, regardless of whether it was previously created.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description value table Table containing column-value pairs to insert or update. dbTable string Optional table name without "lia_" prefix (defaults to "characters" if not specified).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with {results = results, lastID = lastID}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Ensure a character exists with this data
    lia.db.upsert({
        id = 123,
        steamID = "STEAM_0:1:12345678",
        name = "John Doe",
        money = "1000"
    }, "characters"):next(function(result)
        print("Character upserted, last ID:", result.lastID)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Deletes rows from the specified database table that match the given condition.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when removing records from database tables, such as deleting characters or cleaning up old data.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description dbTable string Table name without "lia_" prefix (defaults to "character" if not specified). condition **table string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with {results = results, lastID = lastID}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Delete a specific character
    lia.db.delete("characters", {id = 123}):next(function()
        print("Character deleted")
    end)
    -- Delete all characters for a player
    lia.db.delete("characters", {steamID = "STEAM_0:1:12345678"}):next(function()
        print("All player characters deleted")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a new database table with the specified schema definition.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when creating custom tables for modules or extending the database schema.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description dbName string Table name without "lia_" prefix. primaryKey string Optional name of the primary key column. schema table Array of column definitions with name, type, not_null, and default properties.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves to true when the table is created.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local schema = {
        {name = "id", type = "integer", not_null = true},
        {name = "name", type = "string", not_null = true},
        {name = "value", type = "integer", default = 0}
    }
    lia.db.createTable("custom_data", "id", schema):next(function()
        print("Custom table created")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Adds a new column to an existing database table.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when extending database schema by adding new fields to existing tables.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description tableName string Table name without "lia_" prefix. columnName string Name of the new column to add. columnType string SQL data type for the column (e.g., "VARCHAR(255)", "INTEGER", "TEXT"). defaultValue any Optional default value for the new column.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves to true if column was added, false if it already exists.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.createColumn("characters", "custom_field", "VARCHAR(100)", "default_value"):next(function(success)
        if success then
            print("Column added successfully")
        else
            print("Column already exists")
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Removes a database table from the database.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when cleaning up or removing custom tables from the database schema.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description tableName string Table name without "lia_" prefix.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves to true if table was removed, false if it doesn't exist.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.removeTable("custom_data"):next(function(success)
        if success then
            print("Table removed successfully")
        else
            print("Table does not exist")
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Removes a column from an existing database table by recreating the table without the specified column.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when removing fields from database tables during schema cleanup or refactoring.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description tableName string Table name without "lia_" prefix. columnName string Name of the column to remove.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves to true if column was removed, false if table/column doesn't exist.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.removeColumn("characters", "old_field"):next(function(success)
        if success then
            print("Column removed successfully")
        else
            print("Column or table does not exist")
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves the column information/schema for the characters table.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing to inspect the structure of the characters table for schema operations.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description callback function Function to call with the array of column names.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.getCharacterTable(function(columns)
        print("Character table columns:")
        for _, column in ipairs(columns) do
            print("-", column)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a backup snapshot of all data in the specified table and saves it to a JSON file.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called for backup purposes before major data operations or schema changes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description tableName string Table name without "lia_" prefix.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with snapshot info {file = filename, path = filepath, records = count}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.createSnapshot("characters"):next(function(snapshot)
        print("Snapshot created:", snapshot.file, "with", snapshot.records, "records")
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads data from a snapshot file and restores it to the corresponding database table.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called to restore database tables from backup snapshots.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description fileName string Name of the snapshot file to load.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A promise that resolves with restore info {table = tableName, records = count, timestamp = timestamp}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.db.loadSnapshot("snapshot_characters_1640995200.json"):next(function(result)
        print("Restored", result.records, "records to", result.table)
    end)

docs.title: Derma Library
docs.text:
Advanced UI rendering and interaction system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The derma library provides comprehensive UI rendering and interaction functionality for the Lilia framework. It handles advanced drawing operations including rounded rectangles, circles, shadows, blur effects, and gradients using custom shaders. The library offers a fluent API for creating complex UI elements with smooth animations, color pickers, player selectors, and various input dialogs. It includes utility functions for text rendering with shadows and outlines, entity text display, and menu positioning. The library operates primarily on the client side and provides both low-level drawing functions and high-level UI components for creating modern, visually appealing interfaces.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Opens a fresh liaDermaMenu at the current cursor position and tracks it in lia.gui.menuDermaMenu.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need a standard context menu for interaction options and want any previous one closed first.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The newly created liaDermaMenu panel.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local menu = lia.derma.dermaMenu()
    menu:AddOption("Say hi", function() chat.AddText("hi") end)
    menu:Open()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Builds and shows the configurable options/interaction menu, filtering and categorising provided options before rendering buttons.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when presenting interaction, action, or custom options to the player, optionally tied to a targeted entity and network message.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description rawOptions table Option definitions keyed by id or stored in an array; entries can include type, range, target, shouldShow, callback, and onSelect. config table Optional settings such as mode ("interaction", "action", or "custom"), entity, netMsg, preFiltered, registryKey, sizing fields, and behaviour toggles like closeOnSelect.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel|nil The created frame when options are available; otherwise nil.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.derma.optionsMenu({
        greet = {type = "action", callback = function(client) chat.AddText(client, " waves") end}
    }, {mode = "action"})

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Displays a modal color picker window that lets the user pick a hue and saturation/value, then returns the chosen color.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need the player to choose a color for a UI element or configuration field.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description func function Callback invoked with the selected Color when the user confirms. colorStandard Color Optional starting color; defaults to white.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Operates through UI side effects and the supplied callback.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.requestColorPicker(function(col) print("Picked", col) end, Color(0, 200, 255))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Spawns a radial selection menu using liaRadialPanel and stores the reference on lia.gui.menu_radial.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for quick circular option pickers, such as pie-menu interactions.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description options table Table passed directly to liaRadialPanel:Init, defining each radial entry.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The created liaRadialPanel instance.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.radialMenu({{label = "Yes", callback = function() print("yes") end}})

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Opens a player selector window listing all connected players and runs a callback when one is chosen.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when an action needs the user to target a specific player from the current server list.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description doClick function Called with the selected player entity after the user clicks a card.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The created selector frame stored on lia.gui.menuPlayerSelector.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.requestPlayerSelector(function(pl) chat.AddText("Selected ", pl:Name()) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a rounded rectangle using the shader-based derma pipeline with the same radius on every corner.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when rendering a simple rounded box with optional shape or blur flags.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description radius number Corner radius to apply to all corners. x number X position of the box. y number Y position of the box. w number Width of the box. h number Height of the box. col **Color nil** flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.draw(8, 10, 10, 140, 48, Color(30, 30, 30, 220))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws only the outline of a rounded rectangle with configurable thickness.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need a stroked rounded box while leaving the interior transparent.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description radius number Corner radius for all corners. x number X position of the outline. y number Y position of the outline. w number Width of the outline box. h number Height of the outline box. col **Color nil** thickness **number nil** flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawOutlined(10, 20, 20, 180, 60, lia.color.theme.text, 2)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a rounded rectangle filled with the provided texture.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need a textured rounded box instead of a solid color.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description radius number Corner radius for all corners. x number X position. y number Y position. w number Width. h number Height. col **Color nil** texture ITexture Texture handle to apply to the rectangle. flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local tex = Material("vgui/gradient-u"):GetTexture("$basetexture")
    lia.derma.drawTexture(6, 50, 50, 128, 64, color_white, tex)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Convenience wrapper that draws a rounded rectangle using the base texture from a material.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you have an IMaterial and want its base texture applied to a rounded box.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description radius number Corner radius for all corners. x number X position. y number Y position. w number Width. h number Height. col **Color nil** mat IMaterial Material whose base texture will be drawn. flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawMaterial(6, 80, 80, 100, 40, color_white, Material("vgui/gradient-d"))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a filled circle using the rounded rectangle shader configured for circular output.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need a smooth circle without manually handling radii or shapes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number Center X position. y number Center Y position. radius number Circle diameter; internally halved for corner radii. col **Color nil** flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawCircle(100, 100, 48, Color(200, 80, 80, 255))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws only the outline of a circle with configurable thickness.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for circular strokes such as selection rings or markers.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number Center X position. y number Center Y position. radius number Circle diameter. col **Color nil** thickness **number nil** flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawCircleOutlined(200, 120, 40, lia.color.theme.accent, 2)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a textured circle using a supplied texture handle.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you want a circular render that uses a specific texture rather than a solid color.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number Center X position. y number Center Y position. radius number Circle diameter. col **Color nil** texture ITexture Texture handle to apply. flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawCircleTexture(64, 64, 32, color_white, Material("icon16/star.png"):GetTexture("$basetexture"))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a textured circle using the base texture from an IMaterial.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you have a material and need to render its base texture within a circular mask.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number Center X position. y number Center Y position. radius number Circle diameter. col **Color nil** mat IMaterial Material whose base texture will be drawn. flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawCircleMaterial(48, 48, 28, color_white, Material("vgui/gradient-l"))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Renders a blurred rounded shape using the blur shader while respecting per-corner radii and optional outline thickness.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use to blur a rectangular region (or other supported shapes) without drawing a solid fill.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number X position of the blurred region. y number Y position of the blurred region. w number Width of the blurred region. h number Height of the blurred region. flags **number nil** tl **number nil** Top unknown left radius override. Top unknown left radius override. tr **number nil** Top unknown right radius override. Top unknown right radius override. bl **number nil** Bottom unknown left radius override. Bottom unknown left radius override. br **number nil** Bottom unknown right radius override. Bottom unknown right radius override. thickness **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawBlur(0, 0, 220, 80, lia.derma.BLUR, 12, 12, 12, 12)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a configurable drop shadow behind a rounded shape, optionally using blur and manual color control.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you want a soft shadow around a shape with fine control over radii, spread, intensity, and flags.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number X position of the shape casting the shadow. y number Y position of the shape casting the shadow. w number Width of the shape. h number Height of the shape. col **Color nil flags **number nil** tl **number nil** Top unknown left radius override. Top unknown left radius override. tr **number nil** Top unknown right radius override. Top unknown right radius override. bl **number nil** Bottom unknown left radius override. Bottom unknown left radius override. br **number nil** Bottom unknown right radius override. Bottom unknown right radius override. spread **number nil** intensity **number nil** thickness **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawShadowsEx(40, 40, 200, 80, Color(0, 0, 0, 180), nil, 12, 12, 12, 12, 26, 32)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Convenience wrapper to draw a drop shadow with the same radius on every corner.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need a uniform-radius shadow without manually specifying each corner.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description radius number Radius applied to all corners. x number X position. y number Y position. w number Width. h number Height. col **Color nil** spread **number nil** intensity **number nil** flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawShadows(10, 60, 60, 180, 70, Color(0, 0, 0, 150))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Convenience wrapper that draws only the shadow outline for a uniform-radius shape.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need a stroked shadow ring around a rounded box.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description radius number Radius applied to all corners. x number X position. y number Y position. w number Width. h number Height. col **Color nil** thickness **number nil** spread **number nil** intensity **number nil** flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawShadowsOutlined(12, 40, 40, 160, 60, Color(0, 0, 0, 180), 2)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Starts a chainable rectangle draw builder using the derma shader helpers.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you want to configure a rectangle (radius, color, outline, blur, etc.) through a fluent API before drawing.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number X position of the rectangle. y number Y position of the rectangle. w number Width of the rectangle. h number Height of the rectangle.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Chainable rectangle builder supporting methods like :Rad, :Color, :Outline, and :Draw.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.rect(12, 12, 220, 80):Rad(10):Color(Color(40, 40, 40, 220)):Shadow():Draw()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Starts a chainable circle draw builder using the derma shader helpers.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you want to configure a circle (color, outline, blur, etc.) before drawing it.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number Center X position. y number Center Y position. r number Circle diameter.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Chainable circle builder supporting methods like :Color, :Outline, :Shadow, and :Draw.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.circle(100, 100, 50):Color(lia.color.theme.accent):Outline(2):Draw()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sets or clears a specific drawing flag on a bitmask using a flag value or named constant.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when toggling derma drawing flags such as shapes or corner suppression.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description flags number Current flag bitmask. flag **number string** bool boolean Whether to enable (true) or disable (false) the flag.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Updated flag bitmask.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: flags = lia.derma.setFlag(flags, "BLUR", true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Updates the default shape flag used by the draw helpers (rectangles and circles) when no explicit flag is provided.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use to globally change the base rounding style (e.g., figma, iOS, circle) for subsequent draw calls.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description shape number Shape flag constant such as lia.derma.SHAPE_FIGMA; defaults to SHAPE_FIGMA when nil.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Updates internal default state.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.setDefaultShape(lia.derma.SHAPE_IOS)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws text with a single offset shadow before the main text for lightweight legibility.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need a subtle shadow behind text without a full outline.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description text string Text to draw. font string Font name. x number X position. y number Y position. colortext Color Foreground text color. colorshadow Color Shadow color. dist number Pixel offset for both X and Y shadow directions. xalign number Horizontal alignment (TEXT_ALIGN_*). yalign number Vertical alignment (TEXT_ALIGN_*).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.shadowText("Hello", "LiliaFont.18", 200, 100, color_white, Color(0, 0, 0, 180), 1, TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws text with a configurable outline by repeatedly rendering offset copies around the glyphs.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need high-contrast text that stays legible on varied backgrounds.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description text string Text to draw. font string Font name. x number X position. y number Y position. colour Color Main text color. xalign number Horizontal alignment (TEXT_ALIGN_*). outlinewidth number Total outline thickness in pixels. outlinecolour Color Color applied to the outline renders.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number The value returned by draw.DrawText for the final text render.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawTextOutlined("Warning", "LiliaFont.16", 50, 50, color_white, TEXT_ALIGN_LEFT, 2, Color(0, 0, 0, 200))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a speech-bubble style tooltip with a triangular pointer and centered label.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for lightweight tooltip rendering when you need a callout pointing to a position.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number Left position of the bubble. y number Top position of the bubble. w number Bubble width. h number Bubble height including pointer. text string Text to display inside the bubble. font string Font used for the text. textCol Color Color of the label text. outlineCol Color Color used to draw the bubble outline/fill.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawTip(300, 200, 160, 60, "Hint", "LiliaFont.16", color_white, Color(20, 20, 20, 220))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws text with a subtle shadow using draw.TextShadow, defaulting to common derma font and colors.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when rendering HUD/UI text that benefits from a small shadow for readability.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description text string Text to render. x number X position. y number Y position. color **Color nil** alignX **number nil** alignY **number nil** font **string nil** alpha **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Width returned by draw.TextShadow.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawText("Objective updated", 20, 20, lia.color.theme.text, TEXT_ALIGN_LEFT, TEXT_ALIGN_TOP)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Renders a configurable blurred box with optional border and draws one or more text lines inside it.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for notification overlays, labels, or caption boxes that need automatic sizing and padding.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description text **string table** x number Reference X coordinate. y number Reference Y coordinate. options **table nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number, number The final box width and height.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local w, h = lia.derma.drawBoxWithText("Saved", ScrW() * 0.5, 120, {textAlignX = TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER})

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a textured rectangle using either a supplied material or a material path.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need to render a texture/material directly without rounded corners.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description material **IMaterial string** color **Color nil** x number X position. y number Y position. w number Width. h number Height.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawSurfaceTexture("vgui/gradient-l", color_white, 10, 10, 64, 64)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Calls a named skin function on the panel's active skin (or the default skin) with the provided arguments.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use to defer drawing or layout to the current Derma skin implementation.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Skin function name to invoke. panel **Panel nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any Whatever the skin function returns, or nil if unavailable.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.skinFunc("PaintButton", someButton, w, h)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Smoothly moves a value toward a target using an exponential approach based on delta time.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for UI animations or interpolations that should ease toward a target without overshoot.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description current number Current value. target number Desired target value. speed number Exponential speed factor. dt number Frame delta time.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number The updated value after applying the exponential approach.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: scale = lia.derma.approachExp(scale, 1, 4, FrameTime())

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Returns a cubic ease-out interpolation for values between 0 and 1.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for easing animations that should start quickly and slow toward the end.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description t number Normalized time between 0 and 1.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Eased value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local eased = lia.derma.easeOutCubic(progress)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Returns a cubic ease-in/ease-out interpolation for values between 0 and 1.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you want acceleration at the start and deceleration at the end of an animation.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description t number Normalized time between 0 and 1.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Eased value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local eased = lia.derma.easeInOutCubic(progress)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Animates a panel from a scaled, transparent state to a target size/position with exponential easing and optional callback.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use to show panels with a smooth pop-in animation that scales and fades into place.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description panel Panel Panel to animate. targetWidth number Final width. targetHeight number Final height. duration **number nil** alphaDuration **number nil** callback **function nil** scaleFactor **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Mutates the panel in-place and assigns a Think hook.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local pnl = vgui.Create("DPanel")
    pnl:SetPos(100, 100)
    lia.derma.animateAppearance(pnl, 300, 200, 0.2, nil, function() pnl:SetMouseInputEnabled(true) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Repositions a panel so it remains within the visible screen area with a small padding.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use after moving or resizing a popup to prevent it from clipping off-screen.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description panel Panel Panel to clamp.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Mutates the panel position in-place.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.clampMenuPosition(myPanel)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a rectangular gradient using common VGUI gradient materials and optional rounding.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need a directional gradient fill without creating custom materials.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number X position. y number Y position. w number Width. h number Height. direction number Gradient index (1 = up, 2 = down, 3 = left, 4 = right). colorShadow Color Color modulation for the gradient texture. radius **number nil** flags **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawGradient(0, 0, 200, 40, 2, Color(0, 0, 0, 180), 6)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Splits text into lines that fit within a maximum width using the specified font.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use before rendering multi-line text to avoid manual word wrapping.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description text string Text to wrap. width number Maximum line width in pixels. font **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table, number A table of wrapped lines and the widest measured width.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local lines, maxW = lia.derma.wrapText("Hello world", 180, "LiliaFont.16")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Applies a screen-space blur behind the given panel by repeatedly sampling the blur material.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you want a translucent blurred background behind a Derma panel.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description panel Panel Panel whose bounds define the blur area. amount **number nil** passes **number nil** alpha **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawBlur(myPanel, 4, 1, 200)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws a blur behind a panel and overlays a dark tint to emphasize foreground elements.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for modal backdrops or to dim the UI behind dialogs.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description panel Panel Panel whose bounds define the blur and tint area. amount **number nil** passes **number nil** alpha **number nil** darkAlpha **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawBlackBlur(myPanel, 8, 4, 255, 180)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Applies a blur effect to a specific screen-space rectangle defined by coordinates and size.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use to blur a custom area of the screen that is not tied to a panel.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description x number X position of the blur rectangle. y number Y position of the blur rectangle. w number Width of the blur rectangle. h number Height of the blur rectangle. amount **number nil** passes **number nil** alpha **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.drawBlurAt(100, 100, 200, 120, 6, 1, 180)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Builds a dynamic argument entry form with typed controls and validates input before submission.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when a command or action requires the player to supply multiple typed arguments.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description title **string nil** argTypes table Ordered list or map describing fields; entries can be "string", "boolean", "number"/"int", "table" (dropdown), or "player", optionally with data and default values. onSubmit **function nil** defaults **table nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Operates through UI side effects and the provided callback.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.derma.createTableUI("Players", {
        {name = "name", field = "name"},
        {name = "steamid", field = "steamid"}
    }, playerRows)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Builds a full-screen table UI with optional context actions, populating rows from provided data and column definitions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need to display tabular data (e.g., admin lists) with right-click options and copy support.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description title **string nil** columns table Array of column definitions {name = <lang key>, field = <data key>, width = <optional>}. data table Array of row tables keyed by column fields. options **table nil** charID **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel, Panel The created frame and the underlying DListView.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.derma.createTableUI("Players", {
        {name = "name", field = "name"},
        {name = "steamid", field = "steamid"}
    }, playerRows)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Opens a compact options window from either a keyed table or an array of {name, callback} entries.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for lightweight choice prompts where each option triggers a callback and then closes the window.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description title **string nil** options table Either an array of option tables {name=<text>, callback=<fn>} or a map of name -> callback.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The created options frame.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.derma.openOptionsMenu("Actions", {
        {name = "Heal", callback = function() net.Start("Heal") net.SendToServer() end}
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Draws floating text above an entity with distance-based fade and easing, caching per-entity scales.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use to display labels or names above entities that appear when nearby.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description ent Entity Target entity to label. text string Text to display. posY **number nil** alphaOverride **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Draws directly to the screen and tracks internal fade state.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("PostDrawTranslucentRenderables", "DrawNames", function()
        for _, ent in ipairs(ents.FindByClass("npc_*")) do
            lia.derma.drawEntText(ent, ent:GetClass(), 0)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Shows a modal dropdown prompt and returns the selected entry through a callback.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need the player to choose a single option from a list with optional default selection.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description title **string nil** options table Array of options, either strings or {text, data} tables. callback **function nil** defaultValue **any table

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The created dropdown frame.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.requestDropdown("Choose color", {"Red", "Green", "Blue"}, function(choice) print("Picked", choice) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Prompts the user for a single line of text with an optional description and default value.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for simple text input such as renaming or entering custom values.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description title **string nil** description **string nil** callback **function nil** defaultValue **any nil** Pre unknown filled value for the input field. Pre unknown filled value for the input field. maxLength **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The created input frame.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.requestString("Rename", "Enter a new name:", function(val) if val then print("New name", val) end end, "Default")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Presents multiple selectable options, supporting dropdowns and checkboxes, and returns the chosen set.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for multi-field configuration prompts where each option can be a boolean or a list of choices.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description title **string nil** options table Array where each entry is either a value or {display, data} table; tables create dropdowns, values create checkboxes. callback **function nil** defaults **table nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The created options frame.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.requestOptions("Permissions", {{"Rank", {"User", "Admin"}}, "CanKick"}, function(result) PrintTable(result) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Displays a yes/no style modal dialog with customizable labels and forwards the response.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need explicit confirmation from the player before executing an action.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description title **string nil** question **string nil** callback **function nil** yesText **string nil** noText **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The created dialog frame.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.requestBinaryQuestion("Confirm", "Delete item?", function(ok) if ok then print("Deleted") end end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a dialog with a list of custom buttons and optional description, forwarding clicks to provided callbacks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for multi-action prompts where each button can perform custom logic and optionally prevent auto-close by returning false.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description title **string nil** buttons table Array of button definitions; each can be a string or a table with text, callback, and optional icon. callback **function nil** description **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel, table The created frame and a table of created button panels.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.requestButtons("Choose action", {"Heal", "Damage"}, function(_, text) print("Pressed", text) end, "Pick an effect:")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Shows a small popup question with custom buttons, closing automatically unless a button callback prevents it.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for quick confirmation prompts that need more than a binary choice.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description question **string nil** buttons table Array of button definitions; each can be a string or {text, callback} table.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The created popup frame.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.derma.requestPopupQuestion("Teleport where?", {{"City", function() net.Start("tp_city") net.SendToServer() end}, "Cancel"})

docs.title: Dialog Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive NPC dialog management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The dialog library provides comprehensive functionality for managing NPC conversations and dialog systems in the Lilia framework. It handles NPC registration, conversation filtering, client synchronization, and provides both server-side data management and client-side UI interactions. The library supports complex conversation trees with conditional options, server-only callbacks, and dynamic NPC customization. It includes automatic data sanitization, conversation filtering based on player permissions, and seamless integration with the framework's networking system. The library ensures secure and efficient dialog handling across both server and client realms.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Performs a deep comparison of two tables to detect changes, avoiding infinite loops from circular references.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before syncing dialog data to clients to prevent unnecessary network traffic.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description tbl1 table First table to compare. tbl2 table Second table to compare. checked **table nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if tables are identical, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if not lia.dialog.isTableEqual(oldData, newData) then
        lia.dialog.syncDialogs()
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers or updates an NPC configuration entry for customization panels.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During gamemode initialization to define available NPC configuration options.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string Unique identifier for the configuration. data table Configuration data containing fields like name, order, shouldShow, onOpen, onApply, etc.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The stored configuration table.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.dialog.registerConfiguration("shop_inventory", {
        name = "Shop Inventory",
        order = 5,
        shouldShow = function(ply) return ply:IsAdmin() end,
        onOpen = function(npc) OpenShopConfig(npc) end
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves a registered configuration entry by its unique identifier.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When accessing configuration menus or checking configuration availability.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string The unique identifier of the configuration to retrieve.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil The configuration table if found, nil otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local cfg = lia.dialog.getConfiguration("appearance")
    if cfg and cfg.shouldShow(LocalPlayer()) then
        cfg.onOpen(npc)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves sanitized NPC dialog data by unique identifier.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Server-side when preparing dialog data for clients or internal operations.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description npcID string The unique identifier of the NPC dialog.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil Sanitized NPC dialog data, or nil if not found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local npcData = lia.dialog.getNPCData("tutorial_guide")
    if npcData then PrintTable(npcData) end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Returns the original unsanitized NPC dialog definition including server-only callbacks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Server-side when re-filtering conversation options per-player or rebuilding client payloads.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description npcID string The unique identifier of the NPC dialog.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil Original NPC dialog data, or nil if not found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local raw = lia.dialog.getOriginalNPCData("tutorial_guide")
    if raw and raw.Conversation then
        -- inspect server-only callbacks before sanitizing
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sends sanitized dialog data to a specific client or all connected players.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After dialog registration, changes, or on-demand admin refreshes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client **Player nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
concommand.Add("lia_dialog_resync", function(admin)
        if IsValid(admin) and admin:IsAdmin() then
            lia.dialog.syncToClients()
            admin:notifyLocalized("dialogResynced")
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Broadcasts all dialog data to all connected clients.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After bulk changes, during scheduled refreshes, or maintenance operations.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: timer.Create("ResyncDialogsHourly", 3600, 0, lia.dialog.syncDialogs)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers an NPC dialog definition and optionally synchronizes changes to clients.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During gamemode initialization or when hot-loading NPC dialog data.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string Unique identifier for the NPC dialog. data table Complete NPC dialog definition including Conversation, PrintName, Greeting, etc. shouldSync **boolean nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if successfully registered, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.dialog.registerNPC("quests_barkeep", {
        PrintName = "Barkeep",
        Greeting = "What'll it be?",
        Conversation = {
            ["Got any work?"] = {
                Response = "A few rats in the cellar. Interested?",
                options = {
                    ["I'm in."] = {serverOnly = true, Callback = function(client) StartQuest(client, "cellar_rats") end},
                    ["No thanks."] = {Response = "Suit yourself."}
                }
            }
        }
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Opens an NPC dialog for a player, filtering conversation options based on player permissions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When a player interacts with an NPC entity.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player to open the dialog for. npc Entity The NPC entity being interacted with. npcID string The unique identifier of the NPC dialog type.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("PlayerUse", "HandleDialogNPCs", function(ply, ent)
        if ent:GetClass() == "lia_npc" then
            lia.dialog.openDialog(ply, ent, ent.uniqueID or "tutorial_guide")
            return false
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: Doors Library
docs.text:
Door management system for the Lilia framework providing preset configuration,

Overview

The doors library provides comprehensive door management functionality including preset configuration, database schema verification, and data cleanup operations. It handles door data persistence, loading door configurations from presets, and maintaining database integrity. The library manages door ownership, access permissions, faction and class restrictions, and provides utilities for door data validation and corruption cleanup. It operates primarily on the server side and integrates with the database system to persist door configurations across server restarts. The library also handles door locking/unlocking mechanics and provides hooks for custom door behavior integration.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieve door default values merged with any extra fields provided by modules.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Anywhere door defaults are needed (initialization, schema checks, load/save).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table defaults Map of field -> default value including extra fields. table extras Map of extra field definitions collected via the CollectDoorDataFields hook.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Store door data overrides in memory and sync to clients, omitting defaults.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After editing door settings (price, access, flags) server-side.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description door Entity Door entity. data table Door data overrides.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.doors.setCachedData(door, {
        name = "Police HQ",
        price = 0,
        factions = {FACTION_POLICE}
    })

docs.text: Server

docs.title: Faction Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive faction (team) management and registration system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The faction library provides comprehensive functionality for managing factions (teams) in the Lilia framework. It handles registration, loading, and management of faction data including models, colors, descriptions, and team setup. The library operates on both server and client sides, with server handling faction registration and client handling whitelist checks. It includes functionality for loading factions from directories, managing faction models with bodygroup support, and providing utilities for faction categorization and player management. The library ensures proper team setup and model precaching for all registered factions, supporting both simple string models and complex model data with bodygroup configurations.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers a new faction with the specified unique ID and data table, setting up team configuration and model caching.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during gamemode initialization to register factions programmatically, typically in shared files or during faction loading.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string The unique identifier for the faction. data table A table containing faction configuration data including name, description, color, models, etc.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number, table Returns the faction index and the faction data table.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local index, faction = lia.faction.register("citizen", {
        name = "Citizen",
        desc = "A regular citizen",
        color = Color(100, 150, 200),
        models = {"models/player/group01/male_01.mdl"}
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Precaches model files to ensure they load quickly when needed, handling both string model paths and table-based model data.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called automatically during faction registration to precache all models associated with a faction.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description models table A table of model data, where each entry can be a string path or a table with model information.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return a value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local models = {"models/player/group01/male_01.mdl", "models/player/group01/female_01.mdl"}
    lia.faction.cacheModels(models)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return a value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves faction data by either its unique ID or index number.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description identifier **string number**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The faction data table, or nil if not found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

local faction = lia.faction.get("citizen")
    -- or
    local faction = lia.faction.get(1)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number The faction's team index, or nil if the faction doesn't exist.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

local index = lia.faction.getIndex("citizen")

    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

local classes = lia.faction.getClasses("citizen")

        print("Class: " .. class.name)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description faction **string number**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table An array of player entities who belong to the specified faction.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

local players = lia.faction.getPlayers("citizen")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description faction **string number**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number The number of players in the specified faction.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

local count = lia.faction.getPlayerCount("citizen")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks if a faction belongs to a specific category of factions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description faction **string number** categoryFactions table An array of faction identifiers that define the category.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

        print("This is a law enforcement faction")
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description index number The numeric team index for the faction. name string The display name of the faction. color Color The color associated with the faction. default boolean Whether this is a default faction that doesn't require whitelisting. models table Array of model paths for the faction (optional, uses defaults if not provided).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The created faction data table.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local faction = lia.faction.jobGenerate(5, "Visitor", Color(200, 200, 200), true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

-- Called automatically during faction initialization
    lia.faction.formatModelData()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table An array of category names (strings) defined for the faction's models.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

local categories = lia.faction.getCategories("citizen")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves all models belonging to a specific category within a faction.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description teamName string The unique ID of the faction. category string The name of the model category to retrieve.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table A table of models in the specified category, indexed by their position.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

local models = lia.faction.getModelsFromCategory("citizen", "male")

        print("Model " .. index .. ": " .. (istable(model) and model[1] or model))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves the default character class for a faction (marked with isDefault = true).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id **string number**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The default class data table for the faction, or nil if no default class exists.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

    if defaultClass then

    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
