docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called on the client when determining if a faction should be available for character creation.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description faction **string number**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if the player has access to the faction, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

if lia.faction.hasWhitelist("citizen") then
        -- Show citizen faction in character creation menu
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks whitelist access for a faction on the server side (currently simplified implementation).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called on the server for faction access validation, though the current implementation is restrictive.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description faction **string number**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True only for default factions, false for all others including staff.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

-- Server-side validation

    end

docs.title: Flags Library
docs.text:
Character permission and access control system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The flags library provides a comprehensive permission system for managing character abilities and access rights in the Lilia framework. It allows administrators to assign specific flags to characters that grant or restrict various gameplay features and tools. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server handling flag assignment and callback execution during character spawning, while the client provides user interface elements for viewing and managing flags. Flags can have associated callbacks that execute when granted or removed, enabling dynamic behavior changes based on permission levels. The system includes built-in flags for common administrative tools like physgun, toolgun, and various spawn permissions. The library ensures proper flag validation and prevents duplicate flag assignments.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a flag with description and optional grant/remove callback.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During framework setup to define permission flags.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description flag string Single-character flag id. Single unknown character flag id. Single unknown character flag id. desc string Localization key or plain description. callback **function nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.flag.add("B", "flagBuildMenu", function(client, isGiven)
        if isGiven then
            client:Give("weapon_physgun")
        else
            client:StripWeapon("weapon_physgun")
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Execute flag callbacks for a player on spawn, ensuring each flag runs once.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Automatically when characters spawn; can be hooked for reapplication.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Player whose flags should be processed.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: hook.Add("PlayerSpawn", "ApplyFlagWeapons", lia.flag.onSpawn)

docs.title: Font Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive font management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The font library provides comprehensive functionality for managing custom fonts in the Lilia framework. It handles font registration, loading, and automatic font creation for UI elements throughout the gamemode. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server storing font metadata and the client handling actual font creation and rendering. It includes automatic font generation for various sizes and styles, dynamic font loading based on configuration, and intelligent font name parsing for automatic font creation. The library ensures consistent typography across all UI elements and provides easy access to predefined font variants for different use cases.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Create all registered fonts on the client and count successes/failures.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After registration or config load to ensure fonts exist before drawing.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("RefreshFonts", "ReloadAllFonts", function()
        lia.font.loadFonts()
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a single font definition and create it clientside if possible.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During font setup or dynamically when encountering unknown font names.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description fontName string Unique font identifier. fontData table surface.CreateFont data table.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.font.register("liaDialogHeader", {
        font = "Montserrat Bold",
        size = 28,
        weight = 800,
        antialias = true
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: List all registered font identifiers.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Populate dropdowns or config options for font selection.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Sorted array of font names.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
for _, name in ipairs(lia.font.getAvailableFonts()) do
        print("Font:", name)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Convert a base font name to its bold variant.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When auto-registering bold/shadow variants of LiliaFont entries.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description fontName string Base font name.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Best-effort bold font name.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local boldName = lia.font.getBoldFontName("Montserrat Medium")
    lia.font.register("DialogTitle", {font = boldName, size = 26, weight = 800})

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register the full suite of Lilia fonts (regular, bold, italic, sizes).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On config load or when switching the base font setting.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description fontName **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
concommand.Add("lia_reload_fonts", function()
        local base = lia.config.get("Font", "Montserrat Medium")
        lia.font.registerFonts(base)
        timer.Simple(0.1, lia.font.loadFonts)
    end)

docs.title: Inventory Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive inventory system management with multiple storage types for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The inventory library provides comprehensive functionality for managing inventory systems in the Lilia framework. It handles inventory type registration, instance creation, storage management, and database persistence. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing inventory data persistence, loading, and storage registration, while the client handles inventory panel display and user interaction. It supports multiple inventory types, storage containers, vehicle trunks, and character-based inventory management. The library ensures proper data validation, caching, and cleanup for optimal performance.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers a new inventory type with the specified ID and structure.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during gamemode initialization or when defining custom inventory types that extend the base inventory system.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description typeID string Unique identifier for the inventory type. invTypeStruct table Table containing the inventory type definition with required fields like className, config, and methods.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil No return value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.inventory.newType("grid", {
        className = "GridInv",
        config = {w = 10, h = 10},
        add = function(self, item) end,
        remove = function(self, item) end,
        sync = function(self, client) end
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a new inventory instance of the specified type.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when instantiating inventories during loading, character creation, or when creating new storage containers.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description typeID string The inventory type identifier that was previously registered with newType.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table A new inventory instance with items table and copied config from the type definition.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local myInventory = lia.inventory.new("grid")
    -- Creates a new grid-based inventory instance

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads an inventory instance by its ID, checking cache first and falling back to storage loading.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when accessing inventories by ID, typically during character loading, item operations, or storage access.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id number The unique inventory ID to load. noCache boolean Optional flag to bypass cache and force loading from storage.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A deferred object that resolves to the loaded inventory instance, or rejects if loading fails.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.inventory.loadByID(123):next(function(inventory)
        print("Loaded inventory:", inventory.id)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads an inventory from the default database storage, including associated data and items.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called by loadByID when no custom storage loader is found, or when directly loading from database storage.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id number The inventory ID to load from database storage. noCache boolean Optional flag to bypass cache and force fresh loading.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A deferred object that resolves to the fully loaded inventory instance with data and items.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.inventory.loadFromDefaultStorage(456, true):next(function(inventory)
        -- Inventory loaded with fresh data, bypassing cache
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a new inventory instance with persistent storage initialization.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when creating new inventories that need database persistence, such as character inventories or storage containers.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description typeID string The inventory type identifier. initialData table Optional initial data to store with the inventory instance.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A deferred object that resolves to the created inventory instance after storage initialization.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.inventory.instance("grid", {char = 1}):next(function(inventory)
        -- New inventory created and stored in database
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads all inventories associated with a specific character ID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during character loading to restore all inventory data for a character.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description charID **number string**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Deferred A deferred object that resolves to an array of loaded inventory instances.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.inventory.loadAllFromCharID(42):next(function(inventories)
        for _, inv in ipairs(inventories) do
            print("Loaded inventory:", inv.id)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Permanently deletes an inventory and all its associated data from the database.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when removing inventories, such as during character deletion or storage cleanup.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id number The inventory ID to delete.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil No return value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.inventory.deleteByID(123)
    -- Inventory 123 and all its data/items are permanently removed

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Destroys all inventories associated with a character during cleanup.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during character deletion or when cleaning up character data to prevent memory leaks.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description character table The character object whose inventories should be destroyed.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil No return value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.inventory.cleanUpForCharacter(player:getChar())
    -- All inventories for this character are destroyed

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks for items that no longer fit in an inventory after resizing and moves them to overflow storage.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when inventory dimensions change (like when upgrading inventory size) to handle items that no longer fit.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description inv table The inventory instance to check for overflow. character table The character object to store overflow items on. oldW number The previous width of the inventory. oldH number The previous height of the inventory.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if overflow items were found and moved, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if lia.inventory.checkOverflow(inventory, player:getChar(), 5, 5) then
        -- Items were moved to overflow storage
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers a storage container configuration for entities with the specified model.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during gamemode initialization to define storage containers like lockers, crates, or other inventory-holding entities.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description model string The model path of the entity that will have storage capability. data table Configuration table containing name, invType, invData, and other storage properties.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The registered storage data table.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.inventory.registerStorage("models/props_c17/lockers001a.mdl", {
        name = "Locker",
        invType = "grid",
        invData = {w = 4, h = 6}
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves the storage configuration for a specific model.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when checking if an entity model has storage capabilities or retrieving storage properties.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description model string The model path to look up storage configuration for.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil The storage configuration table if found, nil otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local storage = lia.inventory.getStorage("models/props_c17/lockers001a.mdl")
    if storage then
        print("Storage name:", storage.name)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers a vehicle trunk configuration for vehicles with the specified class.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during gamemode initialization to define vehicle trunk storage capabilities.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description vehicleClass string The vehicle class name that will have trunk capability. data table Configuration table containing name, invType, invData, and trunk-specific properties.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The registered trunk data table with trunk flags set.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.inventory.registerTrunk("vehicle_class", {
        name = "Car Trunk",
        invType = "grid",
        invData = {w = 8, h = 4}
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves the trunk configuration for a specific vehicle class.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when checking if a vehicle class has trunk capabilities or retrieving trunk properties.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description vehicleClass string The vehicle class name to look up trunk configuration for.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil The trunk configuration table if found and it's a trunk, nil otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local trunk = lia.inventory.getTrunk("vehicle_class")
    if trunk then
        print("Trunk capacity:", trunk.invData.w, "x", trunk.invData.h)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves all registered trunk configurations.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when listing all available vehicle trunk types or for administrative purposes.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table A table containing all registered trunk configurations keyed by vehicle class.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local trunks = lia.inventory.getAllTrunks()
    for class, config in pairs(trunks) do
        print("Trunk for", class, ":", config.name)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves all registered storage configurations, optionally excluding trunks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when listing all available storage types or for administrative purposes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description includeTrunks boolean Optional flag to include (true) or exclude (false) trunk configurations. Defaults to true.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table A table containing all registered storage configurations, optionally filtered.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Get all storage including trunks
    local allStorage = lia.inventory.getAllStorage(true)
    -- Get only non-trunk storage
    local storageOnly = lia.inventory.getAllStorage(false)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates and displays an inventory panel for the specified inventory.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when opening inventory interfaces, such as character inventories, storage containers, or other inventory UIs.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description inventory table The inventory instance to display in the panel. parent Panel Optional parent panel for the inventory panel.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel The created inventory panel instance.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local panel = lia.inventory.show(myInventory)
    -- Opens the inventory UI for myInventory

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates and displays two inventory panels side by side for dual inventory interactions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when opening dual inventory interfaces, such as trading, transferring items between inventories, or accessing storage.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description inventory1 table The first inventory instance to display. inventory2 table The second inventory instance to display. parent Panel Optional parent panel for the inventory panels.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table An array containing both created inventory panel instances {panel1, panel2}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local panels = lia.inventory.showDual(playerInv, storageInv)
    -- Opens dual inventory UI for trading between player and storage

docs.title: Item Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive item registration, instantiation, and management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The item library provides comprehensive functionality for managing items in the Lilia framework. It handles item registration, instantiation, inventory management, and item operations such as dropping, taking, rotating, and transferring items between players. The library operates on both server and client sides, with server-side functions handling database operations, item spawning, and data persistence, while client-side functions manage item interactions and UI operations. It includes automatic weapon and ammunition generation from Garry's Mod weapon lists, inventory type registration, and item entity management. The library ensures proper item lifecycle management from creation to deletion, with support for custom item functions, hooks, and data persistence.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves an item definition (base or regular item) by its unique identifier.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing to access item definitions for registration, validation, or manipulation.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description identifier string The unique identifier of the item to retrieve.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table or nil The item definition table if found, nil if not found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local weaponItem = lia.item.get("weapon_pistol")
    if weaponItem then
        print("Found weapon:", weaponItem.name)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves an instanced item by its ID and determines its current location.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing to access specific item instances, typically for manipulation or inspection.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description itemID number The unique ID of the instanced item to retrieve.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table or nil, string A table containing the item and its location ("inventory", "world", or "unknown"), or nil and an error message if not found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local itemData, errorMsg = lia.item.getItemByID(123)
    if itemData then
        print("Item found at:", itemData.location)
        -- Use itemData.item for item operations
    else
        print("Error:", errorMsg)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves an instanced item directly by its ID without location information.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing to access item instances for direct manipulation without location context.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description itemID number The unique ID of the instanced item to retrieve.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table or nil, string The item instance if found, or nil and an error message if not found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local item, errorMsg = lia.item.getInstancedItemByID(123)
    if item then
        item:setData("customValue", "example")
    else
        print("Error:", errorMsg)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves the data table of an instanced item by its ID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing to access or inspect the custom data stored on an item instance.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description itemID number The unique ID of the instanced item to retrieve data from.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table or nil, string The item's data table if found, or nil and an error message if not found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local data, errorMsg = lia.item.getItemDataByID(123)
    if data then
        print("Item durability:", data.durability or "N/A")
    else
        print("Error:", errorMsg)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads and registers an item from a file path by extracting the unique ID and registering it.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during item loading process to register items from files in the items directory.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description path string The file path of the item to load. baseID string, optional The base item ID to inherit from. isBaseItem boolean, optional Whether this is a base item definition.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load a regular item
    lia.item.load("lilia/gamemode/items/food_apple.lua")
    -- Load a base item
    lia.item.load("lilia/gamemode/items/base/sh_food.lua", nil, true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks if an object is a valid Lilia item instance.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called to validate that an object is an item before performing item-specific operations.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description object any The object to check if it's an item.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if the object is a valid item, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local someObject = getSomeObject()
    if lia.item.isItem(someObject) then
        someObject:setData("used", true)
    else
        print("Object is not an item")
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves an inventory instance by its ID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing to access inventory objects for item operations or inspection.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description invID number The unique ID of the inventory to retrieve.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table or nil The inventory instance if found, nil if not found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local inventory = lia.item.getInv(5)
    if inventory then
        print("Inventory size:", inventory:getWidth(), "x", inventory:getHeight())
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Adds a new item rarity tier with an associated color for visual identification.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during item system initialization to define available rarity levels for items.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string The name of the rarity tier (e.g., "Common", "Rare", "Legendary"). color Color The color associated with this rarity tier.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.item.addRarities("Mythical", Color(255, 0, 255))
    lia.item.addRarities("Divine", Color(255, 215, 0))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers an item definition with the Lilia item system, setting up inheritance and default functions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during item loading to register item definitions, either from files or programmatically generated.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string The unique identifier for the item. baseID string, optional The base item ID to inherit from (defaults to lia.meta.item). isBaseItem boolean, optional Whether this is a base item definition. path string, optional The file path for loading the item (used for shared loading). luaGenerated boolean, optional Whether the item is generated programmatically rather than loaded from a file.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The registered item definition table.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Register a base item
    lia.item.register("base_weapon", nil, true, "path/to/base_weapon.lua")
    -- Register a regular item
    lia.item.register("weapon_pistol", "base_weapon", false, "path/to/weapon_pistol.lua")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Queues property overrides for an item that will be applied when the item is initialized.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during item system setup to modify item properties before they are finalized.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string The unique ID of the item to override. overrides table A table of properties to override on the item.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.item.overrideItem("weapon_pistol", {
        name = "Custom Pistol",
        width = 2,
        height = 1,
        price = 500
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads all items from a directory structure, organizing base items and regular items.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during gamemode initialization to load all item definitions from the items directory.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description directory string The directory path containing the item files to load.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load all items from the gamemode's items directory
    lia.item.loadFromDir("lilia/gamemode/items")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a new item instance from an item definition with a specific ID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when instantiating items from the database or creating new items programmatically.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string The unique ID of the item definition to instantiate. id number The unique instance ID for this item.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The newly created item instance.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Create a new pistol item instance
    local pistol = lia.item.new("weapon_pistol", 123)
    pistol:setData("durability", 100)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers a new inventory type with specified dimensions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during inventory system initialization to define different inventory types.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description invType string The unique type identifier for this inventory. w number The width of the inventory in grid units. h number The height of the inventory in grid units.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Register a backpack inventory type
    lia.item.registerInv("backpack", 4, 6)
    -- Register a safe inventory type
    lia.item.registerInv("safe", 8, 8)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a new inventory instance for a character and syncs it with the appropriate player.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when creating new inventories for characters, such as during character creation or item operations.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description owner number The character ID that owns this inventory. invType string The type of inventory to create. callback function, optional Function called when the inventory is created and ready.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Create a backpack inventory for character ID 5
    lia.item.newInv(5, "backpack", function(inventory)
        print("Backpack created with ID:", inventory:getID())
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a new inventory instance with specified dimensions and registers it.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when creating inventories programmatically, such as for containers or special storage.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description w number The width of the inventory in grid units. h number The height of the inventory in grid units. id number The unique ID for this inventory instance.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table The created inventory instance.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Create a 4x6 container inventory
    local container = lia.item.createInv(4, 6, 1001)
    print("Container created with ID:", container.id)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Adds custom override data for weapon items during auto-generation.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during weapon item generation to customize properties of specific weapons.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description className string The weapon class name to override. data table The override data containing weapon properties.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.item.addWeaponOverride("weapon_pistol", {
        name = "Custom Pistol",
        width = 2,
        height = 1,
        price = 500,
        model = "models/weapons/custom_pistol.mdl"
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Adds a weapon class to the blacklist to prevent it from being auto-generated as an item.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during weapon generation setup to exclude certain weapons from item creation.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description className string The weapon class name to blacklist.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Prevent admin tools from being generated as items
    lia.item.addWeaponToBlacklist("weapon_physgun")
    lia.item.addWeaponToBlacklist("gmod_tool")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Auto-generates item definitions for all weapons in the game's weapons list.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during gamemode initialization if auto-weapon generation is enabled.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Generate weapon items (usually called automatically)
    if lia.config.get("AutoWeaponItemGeneration", true) then
        lia.item.generateWeapons()
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Auto-generates item definitions for ammunition entities from compatible weapon mods.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during gamemode initialization if auto-ammo generation is enabled.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Generate ammo items (usually called automatically)
    if lia.config.get("AutoAmmoItemGeneration", true) then
        lia.item.generateAmmo()
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sets data on an item instance by its ID and synchronizes the changes.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when needing to modify item data server-side and sync to clients.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description itemID number The unique ID of the item instance. key string The data key to set. value any The value to set for the key. receivers table, optional Specific players to sync the data to. noSave boolean, optional Whether to skip saving to database. noCheckEntity boolean, optional Whether to skip entity validation.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean, string True if successful, false and error message if failed.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local success, errorMsg = lia.item.setItemDataByID(123, "durability", 75)
    if success then
        print("Item durability updated")
    else
        print("Error:", errorMsg)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a new item instance in the database and returns the created item.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when creating new items that need to be persisted to the database.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description index **number string** uniqueID **string table** itemData table, optional The item data to set on creation. x number, optional The X position in inventory. y number, optional The Y position in inventory. callback function, optional Function called when item is created.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table A deferred promise that resolves with the created item.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Create a pistol in inventory 5 at position 1,1
    lia.item.instance(5, "weapon_pistol", {}, 1, 1):next(function(item)
        print("Created item with ID:", item:getID())
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Deletes an item instance by its ID from memory and/or database.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when permanently removing items from the game world.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id number The unique ID of the item to delete.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Delete item with ID 123
    lia.item.deleteByID(123)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads item instances from the database by their IDs and recreates them in memory.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during server startup or when needing to restore items from the database.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description itemIndex **number table**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load a single item
    lia.item.loadItemByID(123)
    -- Load multiple items
    lia.item.loadItemByID({123, 456, 789})

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates and spawns an item entity in the world at the specified position.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when dropping items or creating item entities in the game world.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string The unique ID of the item to spawn. position Vector The position to spawn the item at. callback function, optional Function called when item is spawned. angles Angle, optional The angles to set on the spawned item. data table, optional The item data to set on creation.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table or nil A deferred promise that resolves with the spawned item, or nil if synchronous.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Spawn a pistol at a position
    lia.item.spawn("weapon_pistol", Vector(0, 0, 0), function(item)
        print("Spawned item:", item:getName())
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Restores an inventory from the database and sets its dimensions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when loading saved inventories from the database.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description invID number The unique ID of the inventory to restore. w number The width of the inventory. h number The height of the inventory. callback function, optional Function called when inventory is restored.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Restore a 4x6 inventory
    lia.item.restoreInv(5, 4, 6, function(inventory)
        print("Restored inventory with", inventory:getItemCount(), "items")
    end)

docs.title: Keybind Library
docs.text:
Keyboard binding registration, storage, and execution system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The keybind library provides comprehensive functionality for managing keyboard bindings in the Lilia framework. It handles registration, storage, and execution of custom keybinds that can be triggered by players. The library supports both client-side and server-side keybind execution, with automatic networking for server-only keybinds. It includes persistent storage of keybind configurations, user interface for keybind management, and validation to prevent key conflicts. The library operates on both client and server sides, with the client handling input detection and UI, while the server processes server-only keybind actions. It ensures proper key mapping, callback execution, and provides a complete keybind management system for the gamemode.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a keybind action with callbacks and optional metadata.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During initialization to expose actions to the keybind system/UI.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description k **string number** d **string table** desc **string nil** cb **table nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Table-based registration with shouldRun and serverOnly.
    lia.keybind.add("toggleMap", {
        keyBind = KEY_M,
        desc = "Open the world map",
        serverOnly = false,
        shouldRun = function(client) return client:Alive() end,
        onPress = function(client)
            if IsValid(client.mapPanel) then
                client.mapPanel:Close()
                client.mapPanel = nil
            else
                client.mapPanel = vgui.Create("liaWorldMap")
            end
        end
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Get the key code assigned to an action, with default fallback.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When populating keybind UI or triggering actions manually.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description a string Action name. df **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number|nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local key = lia.keybind.get("openInventory", KEY_I)
    print("Inventory key is:", input.GetKeyName(key))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Persist current keybind overrides to disk.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After users change keybinds in the config UI.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.keybind.save()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Load keybind overrides from disk, falling back to defaults if missing.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On client init/config load; rebuilds reverse lookup table for keys.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: hook.Add("Initialize", "LoadLiliaKeybinds", lia.keybind.load)

docs.title: Languages Library
docs.text:
Internationalization (i18n) and localization system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The languages library provides comprehensive internationalization (i18n) functionality for the Lilia framework. It handles loading, storing, and retrieving localized strings from language files, supporting multiple languages with fallback mechanisms. The library automatically loads language files from directories, processes them into a unified storage system, and provides string formatting with parameter substitution. It includes functions for adding custom language tables, retrieving available languages, and getting localized strings with proper error handling. The library operates on both server and client sides, ensuring consistent localization across the entire gamemode. It supports dynamic language switching and provides the global L() function for easy access to localized strings throughout the codebase.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Load language files from a directory and merge them into storage.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During startup to load built-in and schema-specific localization.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description directory string Path containing language Lua files.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load base languages and a custom pack.
    lia.lang.loadFromDir("lilia/gamemode/languages")
    lia.lang.loadFromDir("schema/languages")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Merge a table of localized strings into a named language.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When adding runtime localization or extending a language.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Language id (e.g., "english"). tbl table Key/value pairs to merge.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.lang.addTable("english", {
        customGreeting = "Hello, %s!",
        adminOnly = "You must be an admin."
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: List available languages by display name.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When populating language selection menus or config options.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Sorted array of language display names.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
for _, langName in ipairs(lia.lang.getLanguages()) do
        print("Language option:", langName)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Build a cache key for a localized string with parameters.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before caching formatted localization results.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description lang string key string ... (vararg)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local cacheKey = lia.lang.generateCacheKey("english", "hello", "John")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Evict half of the cached localization entries when over capacity.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Automatically from getLocalizedString when cache exceeds maxSize.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if lia.lang.cache.currentSize > lia.lang.cache.maxSize then
        lia.lang.cleanupCache()
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Reset the localization cache to its initial state.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When changing languages or when flushing cached strings.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("OnConfigUpdated", "ClearLangCache", function(key, old, new)
        if key == "Language" and old ~= new then
            lia.lang.clearCache()
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Resolve and format a localized string with caching and fallbacks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Every time L() is used to display text with parameters.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Localization key.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Formatted localized string or key when missing.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local msg = lia.lang.getLocalizedString("welcomeUser", ply:Name(), os.date())
    chat.AddText(msg)

docs.title: Loader Library
docs.text:
Core initialization and module loading system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The loader library is the core initialization system for the Lilia framework, responsible for managing the loading sequence of all framework components, modules, and dependencies. It handles file inclusion with proper realm detection (client, server, shared), manages module loading order, provides compatibility layer support for third-party addons, and includes update checking functionality. The library ensures that all framework components are loaded in the correct order and context, handles hot-reloading during development, and provides comprehensive logging and error handling throughout the initialization process. It also manages entity registration for weapons, tools, effects, and custom entities, and provides Discord webhook integration for logging and notifications.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Include a Lua file with realm auto-detection and AddCSLuaFile handling.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Throughout bootstrap to load framework, module, and compatibility files.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description path string File path. realm **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Force client-only include for a UI helper.
    lia.loader.include("lilia/gamemode/core/ui/cl_helper.lua", "client")
    -- Auto-detect realm from prefix (sv_/cl_/sh_).
    lia.loader.include("schema/plugins/sv_custom.lua")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Include every Lua file in a directory; optionally recurse subfolders.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: To load plugin folders or schema-specific directories.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description dir string Directory relative to gamemode root unless raw=true. raw **boolean nil** deep **boolean nil** realm **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load all schema hooks recursively.
    lia.loader.includeDir("schema/hooks", false, true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Include a directory grouping files by realm (sv_/cl_/sh_) with optional recursion.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Loading modular folders (e.g., plugins, languages, meta) with mixed realms.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description dir string Directory relative to gamemode root unless raw=true. raw **boolean nil** recursive **boolean nil** forceRealm **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load all plugin folders, respecting sv_/cl_/sh_ prefixes.
    lia.loader.includeGroupedDir("schema/plugins", false, true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Check framework and module versions against remote manifests.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During startup or by admin command to report outdated modules.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
concommand.Add("lia_check_updates", function(ply)
        if not IsValid(ply) or ply:IsAdmin() then
            lia.loader.checkForUpdates()
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Logs an error message to the console with proper formatting and color coding.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called throughout the framework when errors occur that need to be logged to the console for debugging purposes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description msg string The error message to display in the console.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return any value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Log an error when a required file fails to load
    lia.error("Failed to load configuration file: config.json not found")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Logs a warning message to the console with proper formatting and color coding.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called throughout the framework when non-critical issues occur that should be brought to attention but don't stop execution.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description msg string The warning message to display in the console.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return any value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Log a warning when an optional dependency is missing
    lia.warning("Optional addon 'advanced_logging' not found, using basic logging instead")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Logs an informational message to the console with proper formatting and color coding.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called throughout the framework to provide general information about system status, initialization progress, or important events.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description msg string The informational message to display in the console.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return any value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Log information during system initialization
    lia.information("Database connection established successfully")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Logs bootstrap/initialization messages to the console with section categorization and color coding.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during the framework initialization process to log progress and status of different loading sections.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description section string The name of the bootstrap section being initialized (e.g., "Core", "Modules", "Database"). msg string The message describing what is being initialized or loaded.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return any value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Log bootstrap progress during initialization
    lia.bootstrap("Database", "Connecting to MySQL server...")
    lia.bootstrap("Modules", "Loading character system...")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sends formatted messages to a Discord webhook for logging and notifications.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when important events need to be relayed to Discord, such as server status updates, errors, or administrative actions.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description embed table A Discord embed object containing message details. Supports fields like title, description, color, timestamp, footer, etc.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return any value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Send a notification when a player achieves something special
    lia.relaydiscordMessage({
        title = "Achievement Unlocked!",
        description = player:Name() .. " unlocked the 'Master Trader' achievement",
        color = 16776960, -- Yellow
        timestamp = os.date("!%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Include and register scripted entities, weapons, and effects under a path.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During initialization to load custom entities from addons/schema.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description path string Base path containing entities/weapons/effects subfolders.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load schema-specific entities.
    lia.loader.includeEntities("schema/entities")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Initialize modules, apply reload syncs, and announce hot reloads.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On gamemode initialize and OnReloaded; supports hot-reload flow.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description isReload boolean true when called from OnReloaded.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Called automatically from GM:Initialize / GM:OnReloaded.
    lia.loader.initializeGamemode(false)

docs.title: Logger Library
docs.text:
Comprehensive logging and audit trail system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The logger library provides comprehensive logging functionality for the Lilia framework, enabling detailed tracking and recording of player actions, administrative activities, and system events. It operates on the server side and automatically categorizes log entries into predefined categories such as character management, combat, world interactions, chat communications, item transactions, administrative actions, and security events. The library stores all log entries in a database table with timestamps, player information, and categorized messages. It supports dynamic log type registration and provides hooks for external systems to process log events. The logger ensures accountability and provides administrators with detailed audit trails for server management and moderation.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a new log type with formatter and category.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During init to add custom audit events (e.g., quests, crafting).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description logType string Unique log key. func function Formatter function (client, ... ) -> string. category string Category label used in console output and DB.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.log.addType("questComplete", function(client, questID, reward)
        return L("logQuestComplete", client:Name(), questID, reward)
    end, L("quests"))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Build a formatted log string and return its category.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Internally by lia.log.add before printing/persisting logs.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client **Player nil** logType string ... (vararg)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string|nil, string|nil logString, category

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local text, category = lia.log.getString(ply, "playerDeath", attackerName)
    if text then print(category, text) end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Create and store a log entry (console + database) using a logType.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Anywhere you need to audit player/admin/system actions.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client **Player nil** logType string ... (vararg)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.log.add(client, "itemTake", itemName)
    lia.log.add(nil, "frameworkOutdated") -- system log without player

docs.title: Menu Library
docs.text:
Interactive 3D context menu system for world and entity interactions in the Lilia framework.

Overview

The menu library provides a comprehensive context menu system for the Lilia framework. It enables the creation of interactive context menus that appear in 3D world space or attached to entities, allowing players to interact with objects and perform actions through a visual interface. The library handles menu positioning, animation, collision detection, and user interaction. Menus automatically fade in when the player looks at them and fade out when they look away, with smooth animations and proper range checking. The system supports both world-positioned menus and entity-attached menus with automatic screen space conversion and boundary clamping to ensure menus remain visible and accessible.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Adds a new interactive context menu to the system that can be displayed in 3D world space or attached to entities.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when creating context menus for world interactions, entity interactions, or any situation requiring a visual menu interface.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description opts table A table containing menu options where keys are display text and values are callback functions. pos Vector or Entity The world position for the menu, or an entity to attach the menu to. onRemove function Optional callback function called when the menu is removed.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number The index of the newly added menu in the menu list.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Create a simple menu for an entity
    lia.menu.add({
        ["Open"] = function() print("Opening...") end,
        ["Close"] = function() print("Closing...") end
    }, entity)
    -- Create a world-positioned menu
    lia.menu.add({
        ["Pickup"] = function() print("Picked up!") end
    }, Vector(0, 0, 0))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Renders all active context menus on the screen with smooth animations, range checking, and mouse interaction highlighting.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called every frame during the HUD/rendering phase to draw all active menus. Typically hooked into the drawing system.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Called automatically by the framework's rendering system
    -- Can be manually called if needed for custom rendering setups
    hook.Add("HUDPaint", "DrawMenus", function()
        lia.menu.drawAll()
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Determines which menu item is currently under the mouse cursor and within interaction range.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when checking for menu interactions, mouse clicks, or determining which menu option the player is hovering over.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number, function or nil Returns the menu index and the callback function of the active menu item, or nil if no active menu item is found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Check for menu interactions in a click handler
    local menuIndex, callback = lia.menu.getActiveMenu()
    if menuIndex and callback then
        lia.menu.onButtonPressed(menuIndex, callback)
    end
    -- Check if player is hovering over a menu
    local activeMenu = lia.menu.getActiveMenu()
    if activeMenu then
        -- Player is hovering over a menu item
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Handles menu button press events by removing the menu and executing the associated callback function.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when a menu button is clicked to process the interaction and clean up the menu.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id number The index of the menu to remove from the menu list. cb function The callback function to execute when the button is pressed.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean Returns true if a callback was executed, false otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Handle a menu button press
    local menuIndex, callback = lia.menu.getActiveMenu()
    if menuIndex then
        local success = lia.menu.onButtonPressed(menuIndex, callback)
        if success then
            print("Menu action executed successfully")
        end
    end
    -- Remove a menu without executing callback
    lia.menu.onButtonPressed(specificMenuId)

docs.title: Modularity Library
docs.text:
Module loading, initialization, and lifecycle management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The modularity library provides comprehensive functionality for managing modules in the Lilia framework. It handles loading, initialization, and management of modules including schemas, preload modules, and regular modules. The library operates on both server and client sides, managing module dependencies, permissions, and lifecycle events. It includes functionality for loading modules from directories, handling module-specific data storage, and ensuring proper initialization order. The library also manages submodules, handles module validation, and provides hooks for module lifecycle events. It ensures that all modules are properly loaded and initialized before gameplay begins.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads and initializes a module from a specified directory path with the given unique ID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during module initialization to load individual modules, their dependencies, and register them in the system.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string The unique identifier for the module. path string The file system path to the module directory. variable string, optional The global variable name to assign the module to (defaults to "MODULE"). skipSubmodules boolean, optional Whether to skip loading submodules for this module.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return a value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load a custom module
    lia.module.load("mymodule", "gamemodes/my_schema/modules/mymodule")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Initializes the entire module system by loading the schema, preload modules, and all available modules in the correct order.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called once during gamemode initialization to set up the module loading system and load all modules.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return a value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Initialize the module system (called automatically by the framework)
    lia.module.initialize()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Loads all modules found in the specified directory, optionally skipping certain modules.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called during module initialization to load groups of modules from directories like preload, modules, and overrides.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description directory string The directory path to search for modules. group string The type of modules being loaded ("schema" or "module"). skip table, optional A table of module IDs to skip loading.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil This function does not return a value.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Load all modules from the gamemode's modules directory
    lia.module.loadFromDir("gamemodes/my_schema/modules", "module")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves a loaded module by its unique identifier.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called whenever code needs to access a specific module's data or functions.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description identifier string The unique identifier of the module to retrieve.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table or nil The module table if found, nil if the module doesn't exist.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Get a reference to the inventory module
    local inventoryModule = lia.module.get("inventory")
    if inventoryModule then
        -- Use the module
    end

docs.title: Network Library
docs.text:
Network communication and data streaming system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The network library provides comprehensive functionality for managing network communication in the Lilia framework. It handles both simple message passing and complex data streaming between server and client. The library includes support for registering network message handlers, sending messages to specific targets or broadcasting to all clients, and managing large data transfers through chunked streaming. It also provides global variable synchronization across the network, allowing server-side variables to be automatically synchronized with clients. The library operates on both server and client sides, with server handling message broadcasting and client handling message reception and acknowledgment.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Determine if a net payload with given args is still fresh in cache.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before sending large net payloads to avoid duplicate work.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Cache identifier. args table Arguments that define cache identity.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true if cached entry exists within TTL.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if not lia.net.isCacheHit("bigSync", {ply:SteamID64()}) then
        lia.net.writeBigTable(ply, "liaBigSync", payload)
        lia.net.addToCache("bigSync", {ply:SteamID64()})
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Insert a cache marker for a named payload/args combination.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Right after sending a large or expensive net payload.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Cache identifier. args table Arguments that define cache identity.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.net.writeBigTable(targets, "liaDialogSync", data)
    lia.net.addToCache("dialogSync", {table.Count(data)})

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Receive chunked JSON-compressed tables and reconstruct them.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: To register a receiver for big table streams (both realms).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description netStr string Net message name carrying the chunks. callback function Function called when table is fully received. Client: function(tbl), Server: function(ply, tbl).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.net.readBigTable("liaDoorDataBulk", function(tbl)
        if not tbl then return end
        for doorID, data in pairs(tbl) do
            lia.doors.updateCachedData(doorID, data)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Send a large table by compressing and chunking it across the net.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: For big payloads (dialog sync, door data, keybinds) that exceed net limits.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description targets **Player table netStr string Net message name to use. tbl table Data to serialize. chunkSize **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local payload = {doors = lia.doors.stored, ts = os.time()}
    lia.net.writeBigTable(player.GetHumans(), "liaDoorDataBulk", payload, 2048)
    lia.net.addToCache("doorBulk", {payload.ts})

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Validate netvar payloads to prevent functions being networked.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Internally before setting globals/locals.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Identifier for error reporting. object any Value to validate for networking safety.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean|nil true if bad type detected.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: if lia.net.checkBadType("example", someTable) then return end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Set a global netvar and broadcast it (or send to specific receiver).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Whenever shared state changes (config sync, feature flags, etc.).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Netvar identifier. value any Value to set. receiver **Player table

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.net.setNetVar("eventActive", true)
    lia.net.setNetVar("roundNumber", round, targetPlayers)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieve a global netvar with a fallback default.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Client/server code reading synchronized state.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Netvar identifier. default any Fallback value if netvar is not set.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if lia.net.getNetVar("eventActive", false) then
        DrawEventHUD()
    end

docs.title: Notice Library
docs.text:
Player notification and messaging system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The notice library provides comprehensive functionality for displaying notifications and messages to players in the Lilia framework. It handles both server-side and client-side notification systems, supporting both direct text messages and localized messages with parameter substitution. The library operates across server and client realms, with the server sending notification data to clients via network messages, while the client handles the visual display of notifications using VGUI panels. It includes automatic organization of multiple notifications, sound effects, and console output for debugging purposes. The library also provides compatibility with Garry's Mod's legacy notification system.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Receives notification data from the server via network message and displays it to the client.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Automatically called when the client receives a "liaNotificationData" network message from the server.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- This function is called automatically when receiving server notifications
    -- No manual calling needed

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Receives localized notification data from the server and displays the localized message to the client.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Automatically called when the client receives a "liaNotifyLocal" network message from the server containing localized notification data.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- This function is called automatically when receiving localized server notifications
    -- No manual calling needed

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sends an informational notification to a client using a localized message key with optional parameters.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Called when you want to send an info-type notification with localized text to a specific client or all clients.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
