docs.title: Option Library
docs.text:
User-configurable settings management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The option library provides comprehensive functionality for managing user-configurable settings in the Lilia framework. It handles the creation, storage, retrieval, and persistence of various types of options including boolean toggles, numeric sliders, color pickers, text inputs, and dropdown selections. The library operates on both client and server sides, with automatic persistence to JSON files and optional networking capabilities for server-side options. It includes a complete user interface system for displaying and modifying options through the configuration menu, with support for categories, visibility conditions, and real-time updates. The library ensures that all user preferences are maintained across sessions and provides hooks for modules to react to option changes.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a configurable option with defaults, callbacks, and metadata.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During initialization to expose settings to the config UI/system.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Option identifier to resolve choices for. name string Display name or localization key. desc string Description or localization key. default any Default value; determines inferred type. callback **function nil** data table Extra fields: category, min/max, options, visible, shouldNetwork, isQuick, type, etc.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.option.add("hudScale", "HUD Scale", "Scale HUD elements", 1.0, function(old, new)
        hook.Run("HUDScaleChanged", old, new)
    end, {
        category = "categoryInterface",
        min = 0.5,
        max = 1.5,
        decimals = 2,
        isQuick = true
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Resolve option choices (static or generated) for dropdowns.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: By the config UI before rendering a Table option.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Array/map of options.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local list = lia.option.getOptions("weaponSelectorPosition")
    for _, opt in pairs(list) do print("Choice:", opt) end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Set an option value, run callbacks/hooks, persist and optionally network it.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: From UI interactions or programmatic changes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string value any

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.option.set("BarsAlwaysVisible", true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieve an option value with fallback to default or provided default.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Anywhere an option influences behavior or UI.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string default any

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local showTime = lia.option.get("ChatShowTime", false)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Persist option values to disk (data/lilia/options.json).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After option changes; auto-called by lia.option.set.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: lia.option.save()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Load option values from disk or initialize defaults when missing.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: On client init or config menu load.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: hook.Add("Initialize", "LoadLiliaOptions", lia.option.load)

docs.title: Player Interaction Library
docs.text:
Player-to-player and entity interaction management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The player interaction library provides comprehensive functionality for managing player interactions and actions within the Lilia framework. It handles the creation, registration, and execution of various interaction types including player-to-player interactions, entity interactions, and personal actions. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing interaction registration and validation, while the client handles UI display and user input. It includes range checking, timed actions, and network synchronization to ensure consistent interaction behavior across all clients. The library supports both immediate and delayed actions with progress indicators, making it suitable for complex interaction systems like money transfers, voice changes, and other gameplay mechanics.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Check if a client is within a usable range of an entity.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before running interaction logic or building interaction menus.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player attempting the interaction. entity Entity Target entity to test. customRange **number nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true if both are valid and distance is within range.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Validate a timed hack action before starting the progress bar.
    local function tryHackDoor(client, door)
        if not lia.playerinteract.isWithinRange(client, door, 96) then
            client:notifyLocalized("tooFarAway")
            return
        end
        client:setAction("@hackingDoor", 5, function()
            if IsValid(door) then door:Fire("Unlock") end
        end)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Collect interaction options for the entity the player is aiming at.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When opening the interaction menu (TAB keybind) to populate entries.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client **Player nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Map of interaction name → data filtered for the target.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Server: send only valid interactions for the traced entity.
    net.Receive("liaRequestInteractOptions", function(_, ply)
        local interactions = lia.playerinteract.getInteractions(ply)
        local categorized = lia.playerinteract.getCategorizedOptions(interactions)
        lia.net.writeBigTable(ply, "liaInteractionOptions", categorized)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Gather personal actions that do not require a target entity.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When opening the personal actions menu (G keybind).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client **Player nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Map of action name → data available for this player.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Filter actions for a character sheet panel.
    local actions = lia.playerinteract.getActions(ply)
    for name, data in pairs(actions) do
        if name:find("changeTo") then
            -- add a voice toggle button
        end
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Transform option map into a categorized, ordered list for UI display.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before rendering interaction/action menus that use category headers.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description options table Map of name → option entry (expects opt.category).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Array containing category rows followed by option entries.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Build an options array with headers for a custom menu.
    local options = lia.playerinteract.getCategorizedOptions(interactions)
    local panel = vgui.Create("liaOptionsPanel")
    panel:Populate(options)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a targeted interaction and ensure timed actions wrap onRun.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Server startup or dynamically when new context interactions are added.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Unique interaction key. data table Fields: onRun, shouldShow, range, target, category,

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.playerinteract.addInteraction("zipTie", {
        target = "player",
        range = 96,
        category = "categoryRestraint",
        timeToComplete = 4,
        actionText = "@tying",
        targetActionText = "@beingTied",
        shouldShow = function(client, target)
            return target:IsPlayer() and not target:getNetVar("ziptied")
        end,
        onRun = function(client, target)
            target:setNetVar("ziptied", true)
        end
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a self-action (no target) and auto-wrap timed executions.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Server startup or dynamically to add personal actions/emotes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Unique action key. data table Fields similar to interactions but no target differentiation.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
lia.playerinteract.addAction("wave", {
        category = "categoryEmotes",
        timeToComplete = 1,
        actionText = "@gesturing",
        onRun = function(client)
            client:DoAnimation(ACT_GMOD_GESTURE_WAVE)
        end
    })

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Push registered interactions/actions and categories to clients.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After definitions change or when a player joins to keep menus current.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client **Player nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if lia.playerinteract.hasChanges() then
        lia.playerinteract.sync() -- broadcast updates
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Determine if interaction/action definitions changed since last sync.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Prior to syncing to avoid unnecessary network traffic.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true when counts differ from the last broadcast.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
if lia.playerinteract.hasChanges() then
        lia.playerinteract.sync()
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Open the interaction or personal action menu on the client.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After receiving options from the server or when keybind handlers fire.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description options table Array of option entries plus category rows. isInteraction boolean true for interaction mode; false for personal actions. titleText **string nil** closeKey **number nil** netMsg **string nil** preFiltered **boolean nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Panel|nil The created menu panel.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
net.Receive("liaSendInteractOptions", function()
        local data = lia.net.readBigTable()
        local categorized = lia.playerinteract.getCategorizedOptions(data)
        lia.playerinteract.openMenu(categorized, true, L("interactionMenu"))
    end)

docs.title: Time Library
docs.text:
Time manipulation, formatting, and calculation system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The time library provides comprehensive functionality for time manipulation, formatting, and calculation within the Lilia framework. It handles time parsing, formatting, relative time calculations, and date/time display with support for both 24-hour and 12-hour (American) time formats. The library operates on both server and client sides, providing consistent time handling across the gamemode. It includes functions for calculating time differences, formatting durations, parsing date strings, and generating localized time displays. The library ensures proper time zone handling and supports configurable time format preferences.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Produce a localized "time since" string from a date stamp or Unix time.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Anywhere UI or logs need relative time (e.g., last seen, ban info).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description strTime **string number** "YYYY unknown MM-DD" style date or Unix timestamp (seconds).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Localized human-friendly delta (seconds/minutes/hours/days ago).

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Build a rich status string for a ban record and a last-seen stamp.
    local function formatBanStatus(ply)
        local lastSeen = lia.time.timeSince(ply:getNetVar("lastSeenAt"))
        local banStarted = lia.time.timeSince(ply:getNetVar("banStart"))
        local banEnds = lia.time.formatDHM(ply:getNetVar("banEnd") - os.time())
        return string.format("%s • banned %s • expires in %s", lastSeen, banStarted, banEnds)
    end
    hook.Add("ScoreboardShow", "ShowBanTiming", function()
        for _, ply in ipairs(player.GetAll()) do
            local status = formatBanStatus(ply)
            chat.AddText(Color(200, 200, 50), ply:Name(), Color(180, 180, 180), " - ", status)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Parse a date/time string in "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" format into a numeric date table.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When converting date strings to components for calculations or storage.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description str string Date/time string in "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" format. Defaults to current date/time if omitted.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Table containing numeric date components: {year, month, day, hour, min, sec}.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Normalize a date string into a timestamp for storage.
    local function normalizeDate(inputStr)
        local parts = lia.time.toNumber(inputStr)
        if not parts.year then return nil end
        return os.time(parts)
    end
    net.Receive("liaSubmitEvent", function(_, ply)
        local eventDateStr = net.ReadString()
        local ts = normalizeDate(eventDateStr)
        if not ts then
            ply:notifyLocalized("invalidDate")
            return
        end
        lia.log.add(ply, "eventScheduled", os.date("%c", ts))
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Return the current date/time as a localized formatted string.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: For HUD clocks, chat timestamps, or admin panels showing server time.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Localized date/time in 24h or American 12h format based on config.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Display server time in a HUD corner, honoring American/24h format.
    hook.Add("HUDPaint", "DrawServerClock", function()
        local text = lia.time.getDate()
        surface.SetFont("liaSmallFont")
        local w, h = surface.GetTextSize(text)
        draw.RoundedBox(4, ScrW() - w - 20, 16, w + 12, h + 8, Color(0, 0, 0, 170))
        draw.SimpleText(text, "liaSmallFont", ScrW() - 14, 20, color_white, TEXT_ALIGN_RIGHT)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Format a duration into days, hours, and minutes.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Cooldowns, jail timers, rental durations, or any long-running countdown.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description seconds number Duration in seconds.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Localized X days Y hours Z minutes.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Show detention duration and remaining parole time in an admin panel.
    local function buildDetentionRow(char)
        local remainingSeconds = math.max(char:getDetainTime() - os.time(), 0)
        local formatted = lia.time.formatDHM(remainingSeconds)
        return string.format("%s (%s)", char:getName(), formatted)
    end
    hook.Add("OnAdminPanelOpened", "PopulateDetentions", function(panel)
        for _, char in ipairs(lia.char.loaded) do
            if char:isDetained() then
                panel:AddLine(buildDetentionRow(char))
            end
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Get the current hour string honoring 12h/24h configuration.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: HUD clocks or schedule checks that need the current hour formatted.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string|number "12am/pm" string in American mode; numeric 0-23 otherwise.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Trigger different ambiance based on configured hour format and time.
    local function applyAmbientByHour()
        local hour = lia.time.getHour()
        local numericHour = tonumber(hour) or (tostring(hour):find("pm") and (tonumber(hour:match("%d+")) % 12) + 12 or tonumber(hour:match("%d+")) % 12)
        if numericHour >= 20 or numericHour < 6 then
            RunConsoleCommand("stopsound")
            surface.PlaySound("ambient/atmosphere/city_silent.wav")
        else
            surface.PlaySound("ambient/atmosphere/underground_hall.wav")
        end
    end
    hook.Add("InitPostEntity", "ApplyAmbientHourly", applyAmbientByHour)

docs.title: Utility Library
docs.text:
Common operations and helper functions for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The utility library provides comprehensive functionality for common operations and helper functions used throughout the Lilia framework. It contains a wide range of utilities for player management, string processing, entity handling, UI operations, and general purpose calculations. The library is divided into server-side functions for game logic and data management, and client-side functions for user interface, visual effects, and player interaction. These utilities simplify complex operations, provide consistent behavior across the framework, and offer reusable components for modules and plugins. The library handles everything from player identification and spatial queries to advanced UI animations and text processing, ensuring robust and efficient operations across both server and client environments.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Finds all players within an axis-aligned bounding box.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need the players contained inside specific world bounds (e.g. triggers or zones).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description mins Vector Minimum corner of the search box. maxs Vector Maximum corner of the search box.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table List of player entities inside the box.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local players = lia.util.findPlayersInBox(Vector(-128, -128, 0), Vector(128, 128, 128))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Locates a connected player by SteamID or SteamID64 and requires an active character.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when commands or systems need to resolve a Steam identifier to a live player with a character loaded.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description steamID string SteamID (e.g. "STEAM_0:1:12345") or SteamID64; empty/invalid strings are ignored.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Player|nil The matching player with a loaded character, or nil if not found/invalid input.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
local ply = lia.util.getBySteamID("76561198000000000")
    if ply then print("Found", ply:Name()) end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Returns all players inside a spherical radius from a point.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use to gather players near a position for proximity-based effects or checks.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description origin Vector Center of the search sphere. radius number Radius of the search sphere.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Players whose positions are within the given radius.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
for _, ply in ipairs(lia.util.findPlayersInSphere(pos, 256)) do
        ply:ChatPrint("You feel a nearby pulse.")
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Resolves a player from various identifiers and optionally informs the caller on failure.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client **Player nil** identifier string Identifier to match: SteamID, SteamID64, "^" (self), "@" (trace target), or partial name.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

local target = lia.util.findPlayer(caller, args[1])

    target:kick("Example")

docs.title: Vendor Library
docs.text:
NPC vendor management system with editing and rarity support for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The vendor library provides comprehensive functionality for managing NPC vendors in the Lilia framework. It handles vendor configuration, editing, presets, and rarity systems for items sold by vendors. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server handling vendor data processing and the client managing the editing interface. It includes support for vendor presets, item rarities, stock management, pricing, faction/class restrictions, and visual customization. The library ensures that vendors can be easily configured and managed through both code and in-game editing tools.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a reusable vendor item preset with validated entries.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During initialization to define canned loadouts for vendors (e.g., weapon dealer, medic).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Unique preset name. items table Map of item uniqueIDs to tables with pricing/stock metadata.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Define a preset and apply it dynamically based on map location.
    lia.vendor.addPreset("gunsmith", {
        ar15 = {stock = 3, price = 3500},
        akm = {stock = 2, price = 3200},
        ["9mm"] = {stock = 50, price = 30}
    })
    hook.Add("OnVendorSpawned", "SetupMapVendors", function(vendorEnt)
        if vendorEnt:GetClass() ~= "lia_vendor" then return end
        local zone = lia.zones and lia.zones.getNameAtPos(vendorEnt:GetPos()) or "default"
        if zone == "Armory" then
            vendorEnt:applyPreset("gunsmith")
            vendorEnt:setFactionAllowed(FACTION_POLICE, true)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieve a preset definition by name.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: While applying presets to vendors or inspecting available vendor templates.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Preset identifier (case-insensitive).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil Item definition table if present.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Clone and tweak a preset before applying to a specific vendor.
    local preset = table.Copy(lia.vendor.getPreset("gunsmith") or {})
    if preset then
        preset["9mm"].price = 25
        preset["akm"] = nil -- remove AKM for this vendor
        vendor:applyPreset("gunsmith") -- base preset
        for item, data in pairs(preset) do
            vendor:setItemPrice(item, data.price)
            if data.stock then vendor:setMaxStock(item, data.stock) end
        end
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Fetch a vendor property from cache with default fallback.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Anywhere vendor state is read (pricing, stock, model, etc.).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description entity Entity Vendor NPC entity. property string Property key from lia.vendor.defaults.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any Cached property value or default.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Build a UI row with live vendor state (including defaults).
    local function addVendorRow(list, vendorEnt)
        local name = lia.vendor.getVendorProperty(vendorEnt, "name")
        local cash = lia.vendor.getVendorProperty(vendorEnt, "money") or 0
        local items = lia.vendor.getVendorProperty(vendorEnt, "items")
        list:AddLine(name, cash, table.Count(items or {}))
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Mutate a vendor property, pruning defaults to keep network/state lean.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During vendor edits (net messages) or when scripting dynamic vendor behavior.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description entity Entity Vendor NPC entity. property string Key to update. value any New value to store; default-equivalent values clear the entry.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Dynamically flip vendor inventory for an event and prune defaults.
    hook.Add("EventStarted", "StockEventVendors", function()
        for _, vendorEnt in ipairs(ents.FindByClass("lia_vendor")) do
            lia.vendor.setVendorProperty(vendorEnt, "items", {
                ["event_ticket"] = {stock = 100, price = 0},
                ["rare_crate"] = {stock = 5, price = 7500}
            })
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Broadcast a vendor property update to all clients.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Server-side after mutating vendor properties to keep clients in sync.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description entity Entity Vendor NPC entity. property string Key being synchronized. value any New value for the property. isDefault boolean Whether the property should be cleared (uses defaults clientside).

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Force sync after a server-side rebuild of vendor data.
    local function rebuildVendor(vendorEnt)
        lia.vendor.setVendorProperty(vendorEnt, "name", "Quartermaster")
        lia.vendor.setVendorProperty(vendorEnt, "factionSellScales", { [FACTION_POLICE] = 0.8 })
        lia.vendor.syncVendorProperty(vendorEnt, "name", "Quartermaster", false)
        lia.vendor.syncVendorProperty(vendorEnt, "factionSellScales", { [FACTION_POLICE] = 0.8 }, false)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Build a full vendor state table with defaults applied.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before serializing vendor data for saving or sending to clients.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description entity Entity Vendor NPC entity.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Key-value table covering every defaulted vendor property.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Serialize full vendor state for a persistence layer.
    net.Receive("RequestVendorSnapshot", function(_, ply)
        local ent = net.ReadEntity()
        local data = lia.vendor.getAllVendorData(ent)
        if not data then return end
        lia.data.set("vendor_" .. ent:EntIndex(), data)
        net.Start("SendVendorSnapshot")
        net.WriteTable(data)
        net.Send(ply)
    end)

docs.title: Web Image Library
docs.text:
Web-based image downloading, caching, and management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The web image library provides comprehensive functionality for downloading, caching, and managing web-based images in the Lilia framework. It handles automatic downloading of images from URLs, local caching to improve performance, and seamless integration with Garry's Mod's material system. The library operates on both server and client sides, with intelligent caching mechanisms that prevent redundant downloads and ensure images are available offline after initial download. It includes URL validation, file format detection, and automatic directory management for organized storage. The library also provides hooks for download events and statistics tracking. Images are stored in the data/lilia/webimages/ directory and can be accessed through various path formats for maximum compatibility with existing code.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Ensure a remote image is downloaded, validated, and made available as a Material.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During UI setup when an image asset must be cached before drawing panels.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description n string Logical storage name for the downloaded image. u **string nil** cb **function nil** flags **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Preload multiple HUD icons, then draw them when ready.
    local function preloadIcons(list)
        if #list == 0 then return end
        local entry = table.remove(list, 1)
        lia.webimage.download(entry.name, entry.url, function(mat)
            if mat then
                hook.Run("WebImageReady", entry.name, mat)
            end
            preloadIcons(list)
        end, entry.flags)
    end
    hook.Add("InitPostEntity", "PreloadHUDImages", function()
        preloadIcons({
            {name = "hud/armor_icon.png", url = "https://assets.example.com/images/armor_icon.png", flags = "noclamp smooth"},
            {name = "hud/health_icon.png", url = "https://assets.example.com/images/health_icon.png", flags = "noclamp smooth"}
        })
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Cache metadata for a URL and optionally download it immediately.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: At startup when the gamemode wants to pre-register UI imagery.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description n string Internal key used to store and retrieve the image. u string The HTTP/HTTPS source URL. cb **function nil** flags **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("GamemodeLoaded", "RegisterIconLibrary", function()
        lia.webimage.register("icons/police.png", "https://assets.example.com/ui/icons/police.png", function(mat)
            if mat then lia.log.add(nil, "webimageCached", "icons/police.png") end
        end, "noclamp smooth")
        lia.webimage.register("icons/medic.png", "https://assets.example.com/ui/icons/medic.png", function(mat)
            if not mat then return end
            hook.Add("HUDPaint", "DrawMedicBadge", function()
                surface.SetMaterial(mat)
                surface.DrawTexturedRect(24, ScrH() - 96, 64, 64)
            end)
        end)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieve a previously cached Material for immediate drawing.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Within paint hooks or derma code that needs a cached texture without triggering a download.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description n string The registered name or derived key. flags **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Material|nil The cached material or nil if it isn't downloaded yet.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Render cached image if available, otherwise queue download and retry.
    local function drawIcon(name, x, y)
        local mat = lia.webimage.get(name, "noclamp smooth")
        if mat then
            surface.SetMaterial(mat)
            surface.DrawTexturedRect(x, y, 64, 64)
        else
            lia.webimage.download(name)
            timer.Simple(0.2, function() drawIcon(name, x, y) end)
        end
    end
    hook.Add("HUDPaint", "DrawPoliceIcon", function() drawIcon("icons/police.png", 32, 32) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Expose download statistics to aid diagnostics or admin tooling.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When reporting the number of cached images or implementing cache health checks.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table { downloaded = number, stored = number, lastReset = timestamp }.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("PlayerSay", "PrintWebImageStats", function(ply, text)
        if text ~= "!imagecache" then return end
        local stats = lia.webimage.getStats()
        ply:notifyLocalized("webImageStats", stats.downloaded, stats.stored, os.date("%c", stats.lastReset))
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Evict all downloaded web images, resetting the material cache.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During configuration reloads or when manual cache management is required.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description skipReRegister boolean When true, previously registered URLs are not re-downloaded.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Drop and re-download web images after admins push new branding.
    hook.Add("OnConfigReload", "RebuildWebImageCache", function()
        lia.webimage.clearCache(false)
        for name, data in pairs(lia.webimage.stored) do
            lia.webimage.download(name, data.url, nil, data.flags)
        end
    end)

docs.title: WebSound Library
docs.text:
Web-based audio content downloading, caching, and playback system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The websound library provides comprehensive functionality for managing web-based audio content in the Lilia framework. It handles downloading, caching, validation, and playback of sound files from HTTP/HTTPS URLs, with automatic local storage and retrieval. The library operates on both server and client sides, providing seamless integration with Garry's Mod's sound system through enhanced versions of sound.PlayFile, sound.PlayURL, and surface.PlaySound functions. It includes robust URL validation, file format verification, caching mechanisms, and statistics tracking. The library ensures optimal performance by avoiding redundant downloads and providing fallback mechanisms for failed downloads while maintaining compatibility with existing sound APIs.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Download or reuse a cached web sound by name, running a callback with the local file path.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Whenever a module needs to ensure a URL-based sound exists before playback, especially during initialization.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string The logical name for this sound; normalized before saving. url **string nil** cb **function nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Nothing.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Preload multiple tracks in sequence, falling back to cached copies.
    local playlist = {
        {"music/combat_theme.mp3", "https://assets.example.com/music/combat_theme.mp3"},
        {"music/stealth_loop.ogg", "https://assets.example.com/music/stealth_loop.ogg"}
    }
    local function preloadNext(i)
        local entry = playlist[i]
        if not entry then return end
        lia.websound.download(entry[1], entry[2], function(path, fromCache, err)
            if not path then
                lia.util.logError("Failed to preload " .. entry[1] .. ": " .. tostring(err))
                return
            end
            if not fromCache then
                lia.log.add(nil, "websoundCached", entry[1])
            end
            preloadNext(i + 1)
        end)
    end
    hook.Add("InitPostEntity", "PreloadMusicPlaylist", function() preloadNext(1) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Register a named URL so future calls can rely on a cached entry and optionally download it immediately.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During gamemode setup when you want to associate a friendly key with a remote sound asset.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Logical identifier used for caching and later lookup. url string HTTP/HTTPS link pointing to the desired sound file. cb **function nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string? Returns the cached path if already downloaded.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("PostGamemodeLoaded", "RegisterUIAudio", function()
        lia.websound.register("ui/alert.wav", "https://assets.example.com/sounds/ui/alert.wav", function(path)
            if path then surface.PlaySound(path) end
        end)
        lia.websound.register("ui/ambient.mp3", "https://assets.example.com/sounds/ui/ambient.mp3", function(path)
            if not path then return end
            timer.Create("UIAmbientLoop", 120, 0, function()
                sound.PlayFile(path, "", function() end)
            end)
        end)
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Lookup the cached filesystem path for a registered web sound when you need to play it immediately.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Within any playback logic that wants to skip downloading and use an already fetched file.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string The normalized identifier previously registered or downloaded.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string|nil Local data/ path to the sound file, or nil if not downloaded yet.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Play from cache or queue a download with a one-time completion hook.
    local function playAmbient()
        local cachedPath = lia.websound.get("ui/ambient.mp3")
        if cachedPath then
            sound.PlayFile(cachedPath, "mono", function() end)
            return
        end
        lia.websound.download("ui/ambient.mp3", nil, function(path)
            if path then sound.PlayFile(path, "mono", function() end) end
        end)
    end
    hook.Add("InitPostEntity", "PlayAmbientCached", playAmbient)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Provide download statistics for diagnostic interfaces or admin reporting.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When showing status panels or logging background downloads.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table { downloaded = number, stored = number, lastReset = timestamp }.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
hook.Add("PlayerSay", "ReportWebSoundStats", function(ply, text)
        if text ~= "!soundstats" then return end
        local stats = lia.websound.getStats()
        ply:notifyLocalized("webSoundStats", stats.downloaded, stats.stored, os.date("%c", stats.lastReset))
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Delete all cached web sounds and optionally trigger re-registration.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During round resets or when you want to force a fresh download of every entry.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description skipReRegister boolean If true, registered entries are not re-downloaded automatically.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Force-refresh all cached sounds when admins reload content packs.
    hook.Add("OnConfigReload", "ResetWebSounds", function()
        lia.websound.clearCache(false)
        for name, url in pairs(lia.websound.stored) do
            lia.websound.download(name, url)
        end
    end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Play the configured button click sound with optional overrides and fallbacks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Whenever a UI element wants to use a consistent button audio cue.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description customSound **string nil** callback **function nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Use a per-skin override and fall back to the global default.
    local button = vgui.Create("DButton")
    button.DoClick = function()
        lia.websound.playButtonSound("https://assets.example.com/sounds/ui/blue_click.wav", function(success, err)
            if not success then
                lia.websound.playButtonSound(nil) -- fallback to default
            end
        end)
    end

docs.title: Workshop Library
docs.text:
Steam Workshop addon downloading, mounting, and management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The workshop library provides comprehensive functionality for managing Steam Workshop addons in the Lilia framework. It handles automatic downloading, mounting, and management of workshop content required by the gamemode and its modules. The library operates on both server and client sides, with the server gathering workshop IDs from modules and mounted addons, while the client handles downloading and mounting of required content. It includes user interface elements for download progress tracking and addon information display. The library ensures that all required workshop content is available before gameplay begins.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Queue a workshop addon for download and notify the admin UI.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During module initialization or whenever a new workshop dependency is registered.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id **string number**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Nothing.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Register a workshop addon dependency
    lia.workshop.addWorkshop("3527535922")
    lia.workshop.addWorkshop(1234567890) -- Also accepts numbers

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Gather every known workshop ID from mounted addons and registered modules.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Once modules are initialized to cache which workshop addons are needed.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Set of workshop IDs that should be downloaded/mounted.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Gather all workshop IDs that need to be downloaded
    local workshopIds = lia.workshop.gather()
    lia.workshop.cache = workshopIds

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Send the cached workshop IDs to a player so the client knows what to download.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Automatically when a player initially spawns.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description ply Player The player entity to notify.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Nothing.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Send workshop cache to a specific player
    lia.workshop.send(player.GetByID(1))

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Determine whether there is any extra workshop content the client needs to download.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before prompting the player to download server workshop addons.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean true if the client is missing workshop content that needs to be fetched.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Check if client needs to download workshop content
    if lia.workshop.hasContentToDownload() then
        -- Show download prompt to player
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Initiate mounting (downloading) of server-required workshop addons.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When the player explicitly asks to install missing workshop content from the info panel.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Nothing.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
-- Start downloading missing workshop content
    lia.workshop.mountContent()

docs.title: Character Meta
docs.text:
Character management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The character meta table provides comprehensive functionality for managing character data, attributes, and operations in the Lilia framework. It handles character creation, data persistence, attribute management, recognition systems, and character-specific operations. The meta table operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing character storage and validation while the client provides character data access and display. It includes integration with the database system for character persistence, inventory management for character items, and faction/class systems for character roles. The meta table ensures proper character data synchronization, attribute calculations with boosts, recognition between characters, and comprehensive character lifecycle management from creation to deletion.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Attribute identifier. default number Fallback value if the attribute is missing.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Attribute level plus stacked boosts.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id **number table**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if recognition is allowed by hooks.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if fake recognition passes custom hooks.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Stores custom data on the character and optionally replicates it.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.text: Server

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: Entity Meta
docs.text:
Entity management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The entity meta table provides comprehensive functionality for extending Garry's Mod entities with Lilia-specific features and operations. It handles entity identification, sound management, door access control, vehicle ownership, network variable synchronization, and entity-specific operations. The meta table operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing entity data and validation while the client provides entity interaction and display. It includes integration with the door system for access control, vehicle system for ownership management, network system for data synchronization, and sound system for audio playback. The meta table ensures proper entity identification, access control validation, network data synchronization, and comprehensive entity interaction management for doors, vehicles, and other game objects.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Plays a sound from this entity, handling web sound URLs and fallbacks.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use whenever an entity needs to emit a sound that may be streamed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description soundName string File path or URL to play. soundLevel number Sound level for attenuation. pitchPercent number Pitch modifier. volume number Volume from 0-100. channel number Optional sound channel. flags number Optional emit flags. dsp number Optional DSP effect index.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True when handled by websound logic; otherwise base emit result.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: ent:EmitSound("lilia/websounds/example.mp3", 75)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Indicates whether this entity is a physics prop.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when filtering interactions to physical props only.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if the entity class is prop_physics.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: if ent:isProp() then handleProp(ent) end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks if the entity represents a Lilia item.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when distinguishing item entities from other entities.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if the entity class is lia_item.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.text: • boolean True if the door reports itself as locked.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Net variable name. value any Value to store and broadcast. receiver **Player nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Local variable name. value any Value to store.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.text: • nil

docs.text: Client

docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: Inventory Meta
docs.text:
Inventory management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The inventory meta table provides comprehensive functionality for managing inventory data, item storage, and inventory operations in the Lilia framework. It handles inventory creation, item management, data persistence, capacity management, and inventory-specific operations. The meta table operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing inventory storage and validation while the client provides inventory data access and display. It includes integration with the item system for item storage, database system for inventory persistence, character system for character inventories, and network system for inventory synchronization. The meta table ensures proper inventory data synchronization, item capacity management, item validation, and comprehensive inventory lifecycle management from creation to deletion.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Retrieves a stored data value on the inventory.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use whenever reading custom inventory metadata.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Data key to read. default any Value returned when the key is missing.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • any Stored value or the provided default.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local owner = inv:getData("char")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a subclass of Inventory with its own metatable.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when defining a new inventory type.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description className string Registry name for the new subclass.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Newly created subclass table.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local Backpack = Inventory:extend("liaBackpack")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sets up inventory defaults; meant to be overridden.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Invoked during type registration to configure behavior.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: function Inventory:configure() self.config.size = {4,4} end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sets up inventory defaults; meant to be overridden.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Invoked during type registration to configure behavior.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: function Inventory:configure() self.config.size = {4,4} end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers a proxy callback for a specific data key.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when you need to react to data changes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Data key to watch. onChange function Callback receiving old and new values.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: inv:addDataProxy("locked", function(o,n) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Returns all items in the inventory matching a uniqueID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when finding all copies of a specific item type.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description uniqueID string Item unique identifier. onlyMain boolean Restrict search to main inventory when true.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Array of matching item instances.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local meds = inv:getItemsByUniqueID("medkit")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers this inventory type with the system.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Invoke once per subclass to set type ID and defaults.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description typeID string Unique identifier for this inventory type.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: Inventory:register("bag")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a new instance of this inventory type.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when a character or container needs a fresh inventory.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Deferred inventory instance creation.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local inv = Inventory:new()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Formats the inventory as a readable string with its ID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for logging or debugging output.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string Localized class name and ID.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: print(inv:tostring())

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Registered inventory type data.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local typeData = inv:getType()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description key string Data key that changed. oldValue any Previous value. newValue any New value.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: inv:onDataChanged("locked", false, true)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Returns the table of item instances in this inventory.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when iterating all items.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Item instances keyed by item ID.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: for id, itm in pairs(inv:getItems()) do end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Collects items of a given type from the inventory.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when filtering for a specific item uniqueID.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description itemType string Unique item identifier to match.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table Array of matching items.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local foods = inv:getItemsOfType("food")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description itemType string Unique item identifier to find.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • table|nil Item instance or nil if none found.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local gun = inv:getFirstItemOfType("pistol")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Checks whether the inventory contains an item type.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use before consuming or requiring an item.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description itemType string Unique item identifier to check.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • boolean True if at least one matching item exists.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: if inv:hasItem("keycard") then unlock() end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Counts items, optionally filtering by uniqueID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use for capacity checks or UI badge counts.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description itemType **string nil**

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Total quantity of matching items.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local ammoCount = inv:getItemCount("ammo")

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Returns the numeric identifier for this inventory.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when networking, saving, or comparing inventories.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number Inventory ID.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: local id = inv:getID()

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Inserts an item into this inventory and persists its invID.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when adding an item to the inventory on the server.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description item Item Item instance to add. noReplicate boolean Skip replication hooks when true.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Inventory The inventory for chaining.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: inv:addItem(item)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Alias to addItem for convenience.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use wherever you would call addItem.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description item Item Item instance to add.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Inventory The inventory for chaining.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: inv:add(item)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description item Item Item instance already inserted.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: inv:syncItemAdded(item)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Creates a database record for a new inventory and its data.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: Item Meta
docs.text:
Item management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The item meta table provides comprehensive functionality for managing item data, properties, and operations in the Lilia framework. It handles item creation, data persistence, inventory management, stacking, rotation, and item-specific operations. The meta table operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing item storage and validation while the client provides item data access and display. It includes integration with the inventory system for item storage, database system for item persistence, and rendering system for item display. The meta table ensures proper item data synchronization, quantity management, rotation handling, and comprehensive item lifecycle management from creation to destruction.

docs.title: Panel Meta
docs.text:
Panel management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The panel meta table provides comprehensive functionality for managing VGUI panels, UI interactions, and panel operations in the Lilia framework. It handles panel event listening, inventory synchronization, UI updates, and panel-specific operations. The meta table operates primarily on the client side, with the server providing data that panels can listen to and display. It includes integration with the inventory system for inventory change notifications, character system for character data display, network system for data synchronization, and UI system for panel management. The meta table ensures proper panel event handling, inventory synchronization, UI updates, and comprehensive panel lifecycle management from creation to destruction.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Registers the panel to mirror inventory events to its methods.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Use when a panel needs to react to changes in a specific inventory.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description inventory Inventory Inventory instance whose events should be listened to.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • nil Only installs hooks.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: panel:liaListenForInventoryChanges(inv)

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.text: Client

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: panel:On("Paint", function(s, w, h) draw.RoundedBox(0, 0, 0, w, h, col) end)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Property name to animate on the panel. speed number Lerp speed multiplier. fn function Predicate returning true when the property should approach 1.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.text: Client

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.text: Client

docs.text: Client

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: Player Meta
docs.text:
Player management system for the Lilia framework.

Overview

The player meta table provides comprehensive functionality for managing player data, interactions, and operations in the Lilia framework. It handles player character access, notification systems, permission checking, data management, interaction systems, and player-specific operations. The meta table operates on both server and client sides, with the server managing player data and validation while the client provides player interaction and display. It includes integration with the character system for character access, notification system for player messages, permission system for access control, data system for player persistence, and interaction system for player actions. The meta table ensures proper player data synchronization, permission validation, notification delivery, and comprehensive player management from connection to disconnection.

docs.title: Item Generators
docs.text: Interactive tools to help you quickly generate Lilia item definitions for different item types.

docs.title: 🔫 Weapons Generator
docs.text: Generate weapon item definitions with ammo tracking, skill requirements, and drop settings.

docs.title: 📦 Stackable Items Generator
docs.text: Generate stackable item definitions for resources, materials, and consumables that can pile up.

docs.title: 🔋 Ammo Generator
docs.text: Generate ammunition item definitions for different weapon types and capacities.

docs.title: 🏥 Aid Items Generator
docs.text: Generate health and armor restoration item definitions for medical supplies.

docs.title: 👔 Outfit Generator
docs.text: Generate clothing and outfit item definitions with bodygroup and PAC3 support.

docs.title: 💥 Grenade Generator
docs.text: Generate explosive grenade item definitions with throw physics and sound effects.

docs.title: 📚 Books Generator
docs.text: Generate readable book item definitions with skill requirements and reading mechanics.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
1. Choose an item type from the list above based on what you want to create
  2. Fill out the form fields with your desired item properties
  3. Click the generate button to create the Lua code
  4. Copy the generated code and paste it into the appropriate file in your gamemode
  5. Save and restart your server for the changes to take effect

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
After generating your code, place the files in these directories:

  • Weapons: gamemode/items/weapons/item_name.lua
  • Stackable: gamemode/items/stackable/item_name.lua
  • Ammo: gamemode/items/ammo/item_name.lua
  • Aid: gamemode/items/aid/item_name.lua
  • Outfit: gamemode/items/outfit/item_name.lua

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Start with basics - Weapons and aid items are great for new servers
  • Use consistent naming - Keep file names descriptive and lowercase with underscores
  • Test inventory space - Different item sizes affect how they fit in player inventories
  • Balance values - Consider your server's economy and gameplay balance

These generators create basic item templates. For advanced features and customization options, refer to the full documentation for each item type.

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Name: Advertisements

Description:

Implements a paid /advert command for server-wide announcements. Messages are colored, logged, and throttled by a cooldown to curb spam.

Main Features
  • Adds a paid /advert command (alias: /advertisement) for server-wide announcements
  • Adds colored broadcast messages across the server
  • Adds message logging for moderation purposes
  • Adds configurable cooldown system to prevent spam
  • Adds configurable pricing system
  • Adds notifications when players lack funds or are on cooldown

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Version 1.5
docs.text:
• Enhanced documentation with detailed configuration options including AdvertPrice and AdvertCooldown settings with usage guidelines
  • Added comprehensive hooks documentation for the AdvertSent hook with multiple complexity examples
  • Improved documentation structure and formatting for better readability

docs.title: Version 1.4
docs.text: • Added comprehensive configuration documentation

docs.title: Version 1.3
docs.text: • Added comprehensive hooks documentation

docs.title: Version 1.1
docs.text: • Created docs

docs.title: Version 1.0
docs.text: • Initial Release

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Advertisements module.

Overview

The Advertisements module lets you set how much it costs to send server-wide messages and how long players must wait between messages. This helps prevent spam while allowing important announcements.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much money players pay to send a server-wide advertisement message.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Int

docs.title: 📊 Range
docs.text: Minimum: 1 Maximum: 1000

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Lower values (1-5) make ads cheap but may cause spam
  • Medium values (10-50) work well for most servers
  • Higher values (100+) make ads expensive and rare

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How many seconds players must wait between sending advertisement messages.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Int

docs.title: 📊 Range
docs.text: Minimum: 1 Maximum: 3600

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Short cooldowns (1-10 seconds) allow quick messages but don't prevent spam
  • Medium cooldowns (20-60 seconds) are recommended for most servers
  • Long cooldowns (300+ seconds) make advertisements rare and special

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Advertisements module for server-wide announcements.

Overview

The Advertisements module provides a paid server-wide announcement system that allows players to broadcast messages to all connected players. It implements spam prevention through cooldowns, message logging for administrative oversight, and colored message formatting for better visibility. The module integrates with the economy system for paid advertisements and includes comprehensive hook support for customizing advertisement behavior, validation, and processing workflows.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a player successfully sends an advertisement message to the server.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After an advertisement has been sent, logged, and displayed to all players.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player who sent the advertisement message string The advertisement message content

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
hook.Add("AdvertSent", "LogAdvertisements", function(client, message)
    print(client:Name() .. " sent an advertisement: " .. message)
end)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
hook.Add("AdvertSent", "TrackAdvertisementStats", function(client, message)
    if not client.advertCount then client.advertCount = 0 end
    client.advertCount = client.advertCount + 1

    -- Track advertisement length
    local stats = client:getNetVar("advertStats", {})
    stats.totalLength = (stats.totalLength or 0) + #message
    stats.count = client.advertCount
    client:setNetVar("advertStats", stats)
end)

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
hook.Add("AdvertSent", "AdvancedAdvertisementSystem", function(client, message)
    -- Store advertisement in database
    local char = client:getChar()
    if char then
        lia.db.insert("advertisements", {
            steamid = client:SteamID(),
            charid = char:getID(),
            message = message,
            timestamp = os.time()
        })
    end

    -- Check for special keywords and trigger events
    local keywords = {"sale", "wanted", "hiring", "selling"}
    for _, keyword in ipairs(keywords) do
        if string.find(string.lower(message), keyword) then
            hook.Run("AdvertKeywordTriggered", client, keyword, message)
        end
    end

    -- Send notification to admins
    for _, admin in player.Iterator() do
        if admin:IsAdmin() then
            admin:notify(client:Name() .. " sent: " .. message)
        end
    end
end)

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Name: AFK Protection

Description:

Comprehensive AFK protection system that automatically detects inactive players, prevents exploitation of AFK players, and integrates with restraint systems. Features configurable AFK detection, admin commands, multi-language support, and protection against various player actions.

Main Features
  • Adds automatic AFK detection based on player activity
  • Adds protection against tying up, arresting, or stunning AFK players
  • Adds configurable AFK timeout duration and enable/disable toggle
  • Adds admin commands for managing player AFK status
  • Adds visual indicators for AFK status in character info and HUD
  • Adds multi-language support for all text elements
  • Adds integration with restraint systems

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the AFK Protection module.

Overview

The AFK Protection module automatically detects when players are away and protects them from being tied up, arrested, or exploited. You can set how long players must be inactive before being marked as AFK, and whether protection is enabled.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How many seconds a player must be inactive before being marked as AFK.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Int

docs.title: 📊 Range
docs.text: Minimum: 30 Maximum: 600

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Short durations (30-60 seconds) detect AFK quickly but may mark players during slow roleplay
  • Medium durations (180-300 seconds) work well for most servers
  • Long durations (300+ seconds) only mark players after extended inactivity

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Turns AFK protection on or off. When enabled, AFK players cannot be tied up or arrested.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Boolean

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: true

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Enabled (true) is recommended - protects players who step away
  • Disabled (false) still marks players as AFK but doesn't protect them

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Name: Alcoholism

Description:

Adds drinkable alcohol that increases a player's intoxication level. High BAC blurs vision and slows movement until the effect wears off.

Main Features
  • Adds alcohol items that raise BAC (Blood Alcohol Content) and gradually wear off
  • Adds screen blur and movement effects that scale with intoxication
  • Adds player notification when reaching DrunkNotifyThreshold
  • Adds configurable BAC settings and degradation rates
  • Adds multiple drink items with varying strength (vodka, whiskey, wine, beer, etc.)

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Version 1.3
docs.text: • Added comprehensive hooks documentation

docs.title: Version 1.2

  • ResetBAC() ? resetBAC()
  • AddBAC() ? addBAC()
  • IsDrunk() ? isDrunk()
  • GetBAC() ? getBAC()

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Alcoholism module.

Overview

The Alcoholism module provides configurable alcohol items and intoxication system settings. These settings control the types of alcohol available, their properties, intoxication effects, degradation rates, and visual effects such as screen blur and motion blur. The module allows customization of alcohol items with varying alcohol by volume (ABV), pricing, and physical properties.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Defines the available alcohol items in the game. Each item has properties including name, model, alcohol by volume (ABV), weight, price, category, and inventory dimensions.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: A table containing multiple alcohol items including vodka, whiskey, wine, beer, rum, gin, tequila, cider, mead, moonshine, absinthe, sake, and soju.

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: Each alcohol item contains: - name (string) - Display name of the alcohol - model (string) - Model path for the item - abv (number) - Alcohol by volume percentage - weight (number) - Weight of the item - price (number) - Purchase price - category (string) - Item category - width (number) - Inventory width - height (number) - Inventory height

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Alcoholism module for managing blood alcohol content (BAC) and alcohol consumption effects.

Overview

The Alcoholism module implements a comprehensive intoxication system where players can consume alcoholic beverages that progressively increase their Blood Alcohol Content (BAC). As BAC rises, players experience visual impairments like screen blurring, movement slowdown, and other debilitating effects. The system includes BAC thresholds for different intoxication levels, automatic metabolism over time, and extensive hook integration for customizing alcohol effects, consumption mechanics, and sobriety recovery processes.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a player consumes an alcohol item.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After a player successfully drinks an alcohol item and their BAC has been increased.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player who consumed the alcohol item Item The alcohol item that was consumed

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called whenever a player's blood alcohol content (BAC) value changes.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After the BAC value has been updated on the server, whether it increased or decreased.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC changed newBac number The new BAC value (0-100)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a player's BAC value increases.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After BAC has been increased but before BACChanged is called.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC increased oldBac number The previous BAC value newBac number The new BAC value

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a player's BAC is reset to zero.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After BAC has been reset, typically when a character is loaded or respawns.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC was reset

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a player's BAC reaches a specific threshold level.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When BAC crosses a defined threshold value (e.g., 30%, 50%, 80%).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player who reached the threshold bac number The current BAC value threshold number The threshold that was reached

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called after a player's BAC has been decreased during the degradation cycle.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After BAC degradation has been processed and the new value has been set.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC decreased newBac number The new BAC value after decrease

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called after a player's BAC has been reset to zero.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After BAC reset has been completed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC was reset

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called before a player's BAC is decreased during the degradation cycle.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before BAC degradation is processed, allowing modification of the decrease rate.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC will decrease bac number The current BAC value before decrease

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called before a player's BAC is increased from consuming alcohol.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before BAC increase is processed, allowing modification of the increase amount.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC will increase item Item The alcohol item being consumed amount number The amount of BAC to add

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil or number - Return a modified amount to override the increase

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called before a player's BAC is reset to zero.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before BAC reset is processed, allowing cancellation of the reset.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC will be reset

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil or boolean - Return false to prevent reset

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server
