About

Name: Auto Restarter

Description:

Schedules automatic server restarts at set intervals. Players see a countdown so they can prepare before the map changes.

Main Features
  • Adds scheduling for automatic restarts using RestartInterval
  • Adds a countdown overlay configurable via RestartCountdownFont
  • Adds syncing of next restart time to clients
  • Adds automatic changelevel when the timer expires
  • Adds network messages to sync the restart countdown

The Auto Restarter module provides configurable settings for automatic server restarts. These settings control the restart interval, countdown display, and font used for restart notifications.

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Auto Restarter module for managing automatic server restarts.

Overview

The Auto Restarter module provides automated server restart functionality with scheduled maintenance cycles and player notification systems. It displays countdown timers to give players advance warning of impending restarts, allowing them to save progress and prepare for the map change. The module includes configurable restart intervals, emergency restart capabilities, and comprehensive hook support for integrating with other systems that need to respond to server restart events.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when the server is about to restart automatically.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Immediately before the server executes the restart command.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description timestamp number The current Unix timestamp when restart is triggered

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called during the countdown period before an automatic restart (within 25% of the restart interval).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Every second when the remaining time is within 25% of the restart interval.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description remaining number The remaining seconds until restart

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a new automatic restart is scheduled.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When the restart timer is initialized or when a new restart time is calculated.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description nextRestart number Unix timestamp of the next scheduled restart

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when the automatic restart process has begun.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After AutoRestart is called, just before the map change command is executed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description mapName string The name of the map that will be loaded

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

Name: Body Group Editor

Description:

Spawns a bodygroup closet where players can edit their model's bodygroups. Admins may inspect others and configure the closet's model.

Main Features

  • Adds the ability to customize its model via BodyGrouperModel configuration
  • Adds menu access that requires proximity or privilege
  • Adds an admin command to view another player's bodygroups
  • Adds a networked menu for editing bodygroups

About

Name: Captions

Description:

Offers an API for timed on-screen captions suited for tutorials or story events. Captions can be triggered from the server or client and last for a chosen duration.

Main Features
  • Adds an API for timed on-screen captions (lia.caption.start())
  • Adds support for both client and server use
  • Adds an easy way to deliver story prompts or tutorial messages
  • Adds admin commands to send captions to players
  • Adds duration control for each caption (default: 5 seconds)
  • Adds network synchronization for caption display

docs.title: Hooks

Overview

The Captions module provides a comprehensive API for displaying timed on-screen text overlays suitable for tutorials, story events, and narrative sequences. It supports both server and client-triggered captions with customizable duration, positioning, and styling. The module includes built-in commands for caption management and extensive hook integration for synchronizing caption events across players and customizing caption behavior.

docs.title: Caption Library
docs.text:
Helpers to show and hide Closed Captions for players or locally on the client.

Overview

The caption library provides a simple API for displaying timed on-screen captions/subtitles. It supports both server-side and client-side usage, allowing captions to be triggered from either realm. Captions are displayed for a specified duration and can be manually stopped before the duration expires. The library is ideal for tutorials, story events, or any scenario where temporary on-screen text is needed.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Begin displaying a caption for a duration.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When temporarily showing on-screen captions/subtitles for tutorials, story events, or notifications.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Recipient (Server variant only) text string Caption text to display duration number Seconds to show the caption

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • void

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server and Client variants exist.

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Show a caption on the client
    lia.caption.start("Welcome", 3)

    -- Simple: Show a caption to a player (Server)
    lia.caption.start(ply, "Sector sweep in progress", 5)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Show caption with dynamic text (Client)
    local message = "Loading " .. data.name .. "..."
    lia.caption.start(message, 2.5)

    -- Medium: Show caption to multiple players (Server)
    for _, ply in ipairs(player.GetAll()) do
        if ply:IsValid() then
            lia.caption.start(ply, "Event starting soon", 5)
        end
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Conditional caption display with validation
    local function showCaption(player, text, duration, condition)
        if not IsValid(player) then return end
        if condition and player:IsValid() then
            local finalText = text
            if player:GetNWBool("isAdmin", false) then
                finalText = "[ADMIN] " .. text
            end
            lia.caption.start(player, finalText, duration or 3)
            hook.Run("OnCaptionShown", player, finalText, duration)
        end
    end
    showCaption(ply, "Mission complete", 5, ply:Alive())

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Stop an active caption before its duration expires.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need to immediately hide a currently displaying caption.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Recipient (Server variant only, optional on client)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • void

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server and Client variants exist.

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Stop caption on client
    lia.caption.finish()

    -- Simple: Stop caption for a player (Server)
    lia.caption.finish(ply)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Stop caption after condition
    if condition then
        lia.caption.finish()
    end

    -- Medium: Stop captions for all players (Server)
    for _, ply in ipairs(player.GetAll()) do
        lia.caption.finish(ply)
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Stop captions with validation and hooks
    local function stopCaptionSafely(player)
        if not IsValid(player) then return end
        if SERVER then
            lia.caption.finish(player)
            hook.Run("OnCaptionStopped", player)
        else
            lia.caption.finish()
            hook.Run("OnCaptionStopped")
        end
    end
    stopCaptionSafely(ply)

Configuration options for the Community Commands module.

Overview

The Community Commands module provides configurable URL links that can be accessed through commands, allowing players to quickly access external resources like rules, Discord, Steam content, and community groups.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Defines URL links accessible through community commands. Each URL entry specifies the link name, URL address, and whether it should open in-game or externally.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["Rules"] = {
        URL = "https://example.com/rules",
        shouldOpenIngame = true
    },
    ["Discord"] = {
        URL = "https://discord.gg/2QyjggCUx4",
        shouldOpenIngame = false
    },
    ["Content"] = {
        URL = "https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/managecollection/?id=3367324267",
        shouldOpenIngame = false
    },
    ["SteamGroup"] = {
        URL = "https://steamcommunity.com/groups/partumverse",
        shouldOpenIngame = false
    }
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping link names (keys) to URL configuration tables (values). Each URL configuration contains: - URL (string) - The web address to open - shouldOpenIngame (boolean) - Whether to open the URL in the in-game browser (true) or external browser (false)

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

About

Name: Cutscenes

Description:

Adds a framework for simple cutscene playback, scenes defined through tables, syncing of camera movement across clients, commands to trigger cutscenes, and the ability for players to skip.

Main Features
  • Adds a framework for simple cutscene playback
  • Adds scenes defined through tables in config.lua
  • Adds syncing of camera movement across clients
  • Adds admin commands to trigger cutscenes
  • Adds the ability for players to skip cutscenes

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Version 1.3

docs.title: Version 1.2

docs.title: Version 1.1
docs.text: • Created docs

docs.text: • Initial Release

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Cutscenes module.

Overview

The Cutscenes module lets you create cinematic scenes with images, sounds, and text. Perfect for server introductions, event announcements, or storytelling.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How many seconds it takes to fade between cutscene scenes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Short delays (0.5-1 second) create quick transitions
  • Medium delays (2-3 seconds) work well for most cutscenes
  • Long delays (4+ seconds) create slow, dramatic transitions

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Defines your cutscenes. Each cutscene can have multiple scenes with images, sounds, and subtitles.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: Example cutscenes including "Mistery Info", "Battle Intro", and "Victory Celebration".

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: Each cutscene contains scenes with: - image (string) - Image URL or file path - sound (string) - Sound file path (optional) - subtitles (table) - Array of subtitle entries with: - text (string) - The subtitle text - font (string) - Font name like "liaSubtitleFont" - duration (number) - How long to show it in seconds - color (Color) - Text color (optional) - songFade (boolean) - Fade out music at end (optional)

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Use web URLs (like Imgur) for images or local file paths
  • Subtitle duration: allow about 1 second per 3-4 words
  • Use "liaSubtitleFont" for regular text or "liaTitleFont" for emphasis
  • Keep cutscenes short so players don't skip them

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Cutscenes module for managing cutscene playback and display.

Overview

The Cutscenes module provides a flexible framework for creating and playing scripted cinematic sequences with synchronized camera movement across all clients. It supports table-defined scenes, player skip controls, administrative commands for cutscene management, and comprehensive hook integration for customizing cutscene behavior and extending cinematic functionality.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a cutscene has completely finished playing.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After the fade-out animation completes and all cutscene elements are removed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene that ended

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a specific scene within a cutscene ends.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After a scene's duration expires and before the next scene starts (or cutscene ends).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene scene table The scene data that just ended

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a specific scene within a cutscene starts.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When a new scene begins playing within the cutscene sequence.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene scene table The scene data that just started

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a cutscene begins playing.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When the cutscene playback is initiated, before any scenes are displayed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene that started ply Player, optional The player who started the cutscene (server-side only)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client (id only) or Server (ply, id)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a subtitle within a cutscene scene starts displaying.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When a subtitle begins showing, including sound playback if configured.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene subtitle table The subtitle data containing text, color, font, and sound

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

Overview

The Damage Numbers module displays floating combat text that shows damage dealt and received during combat encounters. It features color-coded damage types, scaling text based on damage amounts, and client-side toggle options. The module provides real-time visual feedback for combat actions with comprehensive hook integration for customizing damage display behavior and extending combat feedback systems.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Donator module.

Overview

The Donator module lets you give special benefits to donators and user groups. You can set character limits, inventory sizes, group assignments, and weapon access.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Sets how many characters each user group can have.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["founder"] = 10,
    ["superadmin"] = 3,
    ["admin"] = 3,
    ["user"] = 2
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping user group names to character limit numbers.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Add user groups like ["vip"] = 5 to give VIP players 5 characters
  • Higher numbers allow more characters per group

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Links donator groups to benefit groups (like pet access).

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["vip"] = "pet"
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping donator group names to benefit group names.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: ["vip"] = "pet" gives VIP players pet access
  • Add more entries for different donator tiers

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Sets custom inventory sizes for specific user groups.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["superadmin"] = {10, 10}
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping user group names to {width, height} arrays.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Format: {width, height} - example {10, 10} means 10 slots wide and 10 slots tall
  • Larger inventories give groups more storage space

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Gives specific Steam IDs access to certain weapons.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["76561198312513285"] = {"weapon_smg1", "weapon_shotgun", "weapon_rpg", "weapon_pistol"}
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping Steam IDs to arrays of weapon class names.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Find Steam IDs from player profiles or admin tools
  • Add weapon class names like "weapon_pistol" to the list
  • Each Steam ID gets its own list of allowed weapons

Name: Hospitals

Description:

Adds respawning of players at hospitals with support for multiple hospital spawn locations on different maps.

Main Features
  • Adds respawning of players at hospitals
  • Adds support for multiple hospital spawn locations
  • Supports different hospital locations for different maps

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Version 1.0
docs.text: • Initial Release

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Hospitals module.

Overview

The Hospitals module provides configurable hospital respawn locations for different maps. When players die and respawn, they are randomly placed at one of the configured hospital locations for the current map, providing a more immersive medical recovery experience.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Defines hospital spawn locations for different maps. Each map can have multiple hospital locations where players will respawn after death.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: A table containing hospital locations for supported maps (currently rp_nycity and rp_nycity_day).

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: Each map entry contains an array of location objects with: - pos (Vector) - The position vector where players will spawn - ang (Angle) - The angle/direction players will face when spawning

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📝 Example
docs.text:
HospitalLocations = {
    ["rp_nycity"] = {
        {
            pos = Vector(-6129.52, 8877.73, 16.03),
            ang = Angle(-0.5, -176.99, 0)
        }
    }
}

docs.title: 🔧 Customization
docs.text: To add hospital locations for a new map: 1. Add a new entry to the HospitalLocations table with the map name as the key 2. Create an array of location objects, each containing pos and ang 3. Position and angle values can be obtained using in-game tools or map editors

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Load Messages module.

Overview

The Load Messages module shows welcome messages to players when they join the server. You can set different colored messages for each faction.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Welcome messages shown to players based on their faction when they join. Each faction can have its own colored message.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    [FACTION_STAFF] = {Color(255, 215, 0), "Welcome, staff member. Monitor the server responsibly."}
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping faction IDs to arrays containing: - Color object for the message text color - String message text to display

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Use faction constants like FACTION_STAFF, FACTION_CITIZEN, etc.
  • Color examples: Color(255, 215, 0) for gold, Color(100, 150, 255) for blue
  • Keep messages short and informative

Example: 

MODULE.FactionMessages = {
    [FACTION_STAFF] = {Color(255, 215, 0), "Welcome, staff member."},
    [FACTION_POLICE] = {Color(100, 150, 255), "Welcome to the police force."},
    [FACTION_CITIZEN] = {Color(255, 255, 255), "Welcome to the city."}
}

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Name: Map Cleaner

Description:

Adds periodic cleaning of map debris, a configurable interval, reduced server lag, a whitelist for protected entities, and manual cleanup commands.

Main Features
  • Adds periodic cleaning of map debris (prop_physics entities)
  • Adds periodic cleaning of dropped items (lia_item entities)
  • Adds configurable cleanup intervals for items and map debris
  • Adds reduced server lag through automatic cleanup
  • Adds whitelist system for protected entity types
  • Adds enable/disable toggle for the cleanup system
  • Adds warning messages before cleanup (60 seconds prior)

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the NPC Drop module.

Overview

The NPC Drop module lets you set which items NPCs drop when they die. You can set different items for each NPC type with different drop chances.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Defines what items each NPC type drops. Higher numbers mean higher drop chance.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["npc_zombie"] = {
        ["soda"] = 25,
        ["cola"] = 10,
        ["water"] = 50,
        ["beer"] = 15
    }
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping NPC class names to item drop tables. Each item has a weight number - higher weights drop more often.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Use NPC class names like "npc_zombie", "npc_citizen", etc.
  • Use item unique IDs like "soda", "water", etc.
  • Higher numbers = more common drops
  • Example: ["water"] = 50 drops more often than ["cola"] = 10

docs.text: A table containing example spawn positions for maps like "rp_nycity_day" with locations such as "City Center" and "Burgers".

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A nested table structure: - Map name (key) → Location table - Location name (key) → Location config: - pos (Vector) - Spawn position - radius (number) - Spawn radius - maxNPCs (number) - Maximum total NPCs at this location - maxPerType (table) - Maximum NPCs per NPC class name

docs.title: 🌐 Realm

docs.title: ⚙️ Type

docs.title: 📊 Range
docs.text: Minimum: 1 Maximum: 3600

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Rumour module for managing rumour spreading mechanics.

Overview

The Rumour module adds an anonymous rumour chat command, hiding of the sender's identity, encouragement for roleplay intrigue, a cooldown to prevent spam, and admin logging of rumour messages.. It provides comprehensive hook integration for customizing managing rumour spreading mechanics and extending functionality.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called to determine if a player can send a rumour.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After validation but before the rumour is sent.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player attempting to send the rumour rumourMessage string The rumour message

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: boolean - Return false to prevent sending

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text:
Server

Name: Shoot Lock

Description:

Adds the ability to shoot door locks to open them, a quick breach alternative, a loud action that may alert others, and chance-based lock destruction.

Main Features
  • Adds the ability to shoot door locks to open them
  • Adds a quick breach alternative
  • Adds a loud action that may alert others
  • Adds chance-based lock destruction

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Slots module.

Overview

The Slots module lets you configure slot machine gambling settings. You can set the cost to play, how often players win jackpots, and how much money they win for different symbol combinations.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much money players pay to spin the slot machine once.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Lower values (1-10) make gambling cheap and accessible
  • Medium values (10-50) work well for most servers
  • Higher values (50+) create expensive, high-stakes gambling

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: The percentage chance of winning a jackpot. Higher numbers mean more frequent jackpots.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Low (1-10%): Rare but exciting jackpots
  • Medium (20-40%): Balanced, recommended for most servers
  • High (50%+): Very common jackpots, may be too easy

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much players win when they get three bar clover symbols. This is multiplied by the bet amount.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: Bet of 13 × 200 = 2,600 currency win
  • This is a high-value win, so use a large multiplier

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much players win for the single bar dollar sign combination.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: Bet of 13 × 50 = 650 currency win
  • This gives players small wins to keep them playing

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much players win for the lucky 7 diamond combination. This is the biggest jackpot.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: Bet of 13 × 500 = 6,500 currency win
  • This should be the highest multiplier since it's the best combination

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much players win for the horseshoe double bar combination.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: Bet of 13 × 100 = 1,300 currency win
  • This is a medium-value win between small and jackpot wins

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Slow Weapons module.

Overview

The Slow Weapons module makes players move slower when holding heavy weapons. You can set different speed penalties for each weapon.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Sets movement speed for specific weapons. Lower numbers mean slower movement.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["fo3_fatman"] = 130
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping weapon class names to movement speed values.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Normal movement speed is usually 200-250
  • Lower values (100-150) make weapons feel heavy
  • Example: ["weapon_rpg"] = 120 makes RPG users move slower

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Name: Steam Group Rewards

Description:

Provides Steam group membership rewards system that automatically checks group membership and gives money rewards to players who join your Steam group.

Main Features
  • Automatic Steam group membership checking every 5 minutes
  • Manual membership verification on demand
  • Configurable money rewards for group members
  • One-time claim per character system
  • Player commands for easy group access and reward claiming
  • Server-side tracking of claimed rewards
Configuration
  • GroupID: Your Steam group ID/name (set in module.lua)
  • MoneyReward: Amount of money to reward players (default: 500, set in module.lua)
Player Commands
  • /group: Opens your Steam group page in the player's browser
  • /claim: Claims the group membership reward (if eligible)
Setup Instructions
  1. Set your Steam group ID in module.lua (line 8): MODULE.GroupID = "yourgroupname"
  2. Optionally adjust the money reward amount (line 9): MODULE.MoneyReward = 500
  3. Players use /group to join your Steam group
  4. After joining, players use /claim to receive their reward
  5. The system automatically checks group membership every 5 minutes

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Steam Group Rewards module.

Overview

The Steam Group Rewards module automatically gives money to players who join your Steam group. You can set which group to check and how much money to give.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: The Steam group ID to check. Players in this group will receive rewards.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: String

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Find your Steam group ID from the group's URL
  • Example: If your group URL is steamcommunity.com/groups/myserver, the ID is myserver
  • Leave empty ("") to disable rewards

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much money to give players who are in your Steam group.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Set this to match your server's economy
  • Players receive this amount when they join the group
  • Higher values give bigger rewards but may affect economy balance

docs.title: Version 1.4
docs.text:
• Added comprehensive hooks documentation
  • Added libraries documentation

docs.title: Version 1.2
docs.text:
• Updated function naming convention from PascalCase to camelCase for consistency:
  • SpeedTest → speedTest
  • DaysBetween → daysBetween
  • LerpHSV → lerpHSV
  • Darken → darken
  • LerpColor → lerpColor
  • Blend → blend
  • Rainbow → rainbow
  • ColorCycle → colorCycle
  • ColorToHex → colorToHex
  • Lighten → lighten

  • SerializeVector → serializeVector
  • DeserializeVector → deserializeVector
  • SerializeAngle → serializeAngle
  • DeserializeAngle → deserializeAngle

Overview

The Utilities module adds extra helper functions in lia.util, simplified utilities for common scripting tasks, a central library used by other modules, utilities for networking data, and shared constants for modules.. docs.title: Utilities Library
docs.text:
Utility helpers for colors, time, math, debug printing, and simple entity spawning.

Overview

The utilities library provides a comprehensive collection of helper functions for common scripting tasks in the Lilia framework. It includes utilities for color manipulation (HSV interpolation, blending, darkening, lightening), time and date operations (formatting, conversion, difference calculations), vector and angle serialization, debug printing, and server-side entity spawning. The library operates primarily in the shared realm, making functions available on both client and server, with some server-specific functions for entity management. It serves as a central library used by other modules and provides shared constants and utilities for networking data.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description func function Function to call repeatedly n number Number of iterations to run

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number - Elapsed time in seconds per iteration

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Benchmark a function
    local took = lia.utilities.speedTest(function() end, 1e5)
    print(took)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Compare function performance
    local function testFunc()
        local sum = 0
        for i = 1, 100 do sum = sum + i end
    end
    local avgTime = lia.utilities.speedTest(testFunc, 1000)
    print("Average time: " .. avgTime .. " seconds")

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Benchmark multiple functions with analysis
    local functions = {
        {name = "Function A", func = functionA},
        {name = "Function B", func = functionB}
    }
    for _, data in ipairs(functions) do
        local time = lia.utilities.speedTest(data.func, 1e5)
        print(data.name .. " took " .. time .. " seconds per iteration")
    end

            good = Color(0, 255, 0),
            warning = Color(255, 255, 0),

-- Medium: Animate color over time
    local function getAnimatedColor(t)
        return lia.utilities.lerpColor(
            math.sin(t) * 0.5 + 0.5,
            Color(255, 0, 0),
            Color(0, 255, 0)
        )
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Multi-color gradient system
    local function getGradientColor(colors, progress)
        progress = math.Clamp(progress, 0, 1)
        local segment = 1 / (#colors - 1)
        local index = math.floor(progress / segment) + 1
        index = math.min(index, #colors - 1)
        local localProgress = (progress % segment) / segment
        return lia.utilities.lerpColor(localProgress, colors[index], colors[index + 1])
    end

-- High: Generate color palette
    local function generatePalette(baseR, baseG, baseB, count)
        local palette = {}
        for i = 1, count do
            local factor = i / count
            palette[i] = lia.utilities.rgb(
                baseR * factor,
                baseG * factor,
                baseB * factor
            )
        end
        return palette
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description sec number Total seconds to convert

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number, number, number, number - Days, hours, minutes, seconds

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity

-- Simple: Convert seconds to components
    local d, h, m, s = lia.utilities.secondsToDHMS(90061)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Format time remaining
    local function formatTimeRemaining(seconds)
        local d, h, m, s = lia.utilities.secondsToDHMS(seconds)
        local parts = {}
        if d > 0 then table.insert(parts, d .. "d") end
        if h > 0 then table.insert(parts, h .. "h") end
        if m > 0 then table.insert(parts, m .. "m") end
        if s > 0 then table.insert(parts, s .. "s") end
        return table.concat(parts, " ")
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Multi-time formatting with localization
    local function formatTimes(times)
        local formatted = {}
        for i, seconds in ipairs(times) do
            local d, h, m, s = lia.utilities.secondsToDHMS(seconds)
            formatted[i] = {
                days = d,
                hours = h,
                minutes = m,
                seconds = s,
                formatted = string.format("%dd %dh %dm %ds", d, h, m, s)
            }
        end
        return formatted
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Return the current local time as a formatted string.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need the current time in a formatted string format.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: None

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string - Current time formatted as "HH:MM:SS - DD/MM/YYYY"

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Get current time
    local time = lia.utilities.currentLocalTime()

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Log with timestamp
    local function log(message)
        local timestamp = lia.utilities.currentLocalTime()
        print("[" .. timestamp .. "] " .. message)
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Comprehensive logging system
    local function logEvent(eventType, data)
        local timestamp = lia.utilities.currentLocalTime()
        local logEntry = {
            timestamp = timestamp,
            type = eventType,
            data = data,
            player = IsValid(ply) and ply:SteamID() or "SERVER"
        }
        table.insert(logSystem, logEntry)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Return a human-friendly difference between now and a time string.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need to calculate and display time differences.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description str string Time string (HH:MM:SS - DD/MM/YYYY)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number - Days difference

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Get days difference
    local days = lia.utilities.timeDifference("14:30:00 - 15/01/2024")

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Check if within time window
    local function isWithinDays(timeString, maxDays)
        local days = lia.utilities.timeDifference(timeString)
        return math.abs(days) <= maxDays
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Activity tracking system
    local function checkPlayerActivity(steamID)
        local lastSeen = GetPData(steamID, "lastSeen", "")
        if lastSeen == "" then return nil end
        local daysAgo = lia.utilities.timeDifference(lastSeen)
        return {
            steamID = steamID,
            lastSeen = lastSeen,
            daysAgo = daysAgo,
            active = daysAgo <= 30
        }
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Serialize a Vector object to JSON format.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need to store or transmit vector data as JSON.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description v Vector Vector to serialize

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • string - JSON string representation of the vector

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Serialize a vector
    local json = lia.utilities.serializeVector(Vector(100, 200, 300))

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Save player position
    local function savePlayerPosition(player)
        local pos = player:GetPos()
        local json = lia.utilities.serializeVector(pos)
        player:SetPData("lastPosition", json)
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Save multiple positions
    local function saveCheckpoints(checkpoints)
        local data = {}
        for i, checkpoint in ipairs(checkpoints) do
            data[i] = lia.utilities.serializeVector(checkpoint.pos)
        end
        return util.TableToJSON(data)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Deserialize JSON data into a Vector object.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need to reconstruct a vector from JSON data.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description data string JSON string to deserialize

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Vector - Reconstructed vector

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Deserialize a vector
    local json = "[100,200,300]"
    local vec = lia.utilities.deserializeVector(json)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Load player position
    local function loadPlayerPosition(player)
        local json = player:GetPData("lastPosition", "")
        if json ~= "" then
            local pos = lia.utilities.deserializeVector(json)
            player:SetPos(pos)
        end
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Load checkpoint system
    local function loadCheckpoints(jsonData)
        local data = util.JSONToTable(jsonData)
        local checkpoints = {}
        for i, vecJson in ipairs(data) do
            checkpoints[i] = {
                pos = lia.utilities.deserializeVector(vecJson),
                id = i
            }
        end
        return checkpoints
    end

    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Spawn a physics prop with optional force, lifetime, angle, and color.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When spawning temporary props server-side for effects or gameplay.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description model string Model path for the prop pos Vector Position to spawn at force Vector|nil Optional force to apply life number|nil Optional auto-remove after seconds ang Angle|nil Optional spawn angle col Color|nil Optional collision group override

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Entity - The spawned entity

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Spawn a prop
    local ent = lia.utilities.spawnProp("models/props_c17/oildrum001.mdl", Vector(0,0,64))

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Spawn prop with force and lifetime
    local ent = lia.utilities.spawnProp(
        "models/props_junk/wood_crate001a.mdl",
        Vector(0, 0, 100),
        Vector(0, 0, 500),
        10,
        Angle(0, 45, 0)
    )

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Spawn prop system with validation
    local function spawnPropSafe(model, pos, options)
        options = options or {}
        if not util.IsValidModel(model) then
            error("Invalid model: " .. model)
            return nil
        end
        local ent = lia.utilities.spawnProp(
            model,
            pos,
            options.force,
            options.lifetime,
            options.angle,
            options.collisionGroup
        )
        if IsValid(ent) and options.color then
            ent:SetColor(options.color)
        end
        return ent
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Spawn multiple entities from a class->properties table.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need to spawn multiple entities at once from a configuration table.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description tbl table Map of entity class to properties (position or full property table)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • void

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Spawn entities
    lia.utilities.spawnEntities({
        ["prop_physics"] = { model = "models/props_junk/wood_crate001a.mdl", pos = Vector(0,0,64) }
    })

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Spawn multiple entity types
    lia.utilities.spawnEntities({
        ["prop_physics"] = Vector(0, 0, 64),
        ["prop_dynamic"] = Vector(100, 0, 64),
        ["npc_combine_s"] = Vector(200, 0, 64)
    })

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Spawn system with full configuration
    local function spawnMapEntities(mapData)
        local entities = {}
        for class, config in pairs(mapData.entities) do
            if istable(config) then
                entities[class] = {
                    pos = config.pos or Vector(0,0,0),
                    ang = config.ang or Angle(0,0,0),
                    model = config.model,
                    keyvalues = config.keyvalues or {}
                }
            else
                entities[class] = config
            end
        end
        lia.utilities.spawnEntities(entities)
    end

Overview

The War Table module provides configurable workshop content ID for the war table model used in-game.

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the War Table module for managing strategic war table functionality.

Overview

The War Table module provides an interactive 3D strategic planning system that allows players to visualize and coordinate operations on detailed maps. It includes marker placement for tactical positioning, support for multiple map layouts, and comprehensive hook integration for customizing gameplay mechanics. The module enables real-time strategic coordination with visual markers, map management, and event-driven interactions that can be extended through custom hooks for specialized gameplay modes, team coordination, and dynamic mission planning.

Name: Word Filter

Description:

Adds chat word filtering, blocking of banned phrases, an easy-to-extend list, and admin commands to modify the list.

Main Features
  • Adds chat word filtering
  • Adds blocking of banned phrases
  • Adds an easy-to-extend list
  • Adds admin commands to modify the list

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Word Filter module.

Overview

The Word Filter module blocks inappropriate words from chat messages. Any message containing a blacklisted word will be completely blocked and not shown to other players.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: A list of words that will be blocked from chat. Messages containing these words won't be displayed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: {"nigger", "faggot", "chink", "kike", "spic", "gook", "wetback", "dyke", "tranny", "retard", "coon", "raghead", "nip", "honky", "gyp", "beaner", "paki", "slant", "slope", "jap"}

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• The filter is case-insensitive (catches "Word", "WORD", and "word")
  • To add words, add them to the list: "new", "inappropriate", "word"
  • To remove words, delete them from the list
  • Customize this list to match your server's rules

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Word Filter module for managing word filtering in chat.

Overview

The Word Filter module adds chat word filtering, blocking of banned phrases, an easy-to-extend list, and admin commands to modify the list.. It provides comprehensive hook integration for customizing managing word filtering in chat and extending functionality.

Overview

The Word Filter module adds chat word filtering, blocking of banned phrases, an easy-to-extend list, and admin commands to modify the list.. It provides comprehensive hook integration for customizing managing word filtering in chat and extending functionality.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called after the filter check completes.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After all filtering is done, regardless of result.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description ply Player The player whose message was checked text string The message text passed boolean Whether the check passed (true) or failed (false)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil
